job_title: Digital Marketing Copywriter Contractor (Remote in US)
job_description:
A Copywriter Contractor at Logical Media Group will be responsible for content creation, primarily focused on creating and developing strategy for copy with Search Engine Optimization (SEO) best practices in mind in order to boost visibility of client marketing efforts and Logical Media Group internal marketing efforts, particularly in search engine ranking, paid media, and email marketing. This will include site research, keyword research, content development, and optimization for our clients.

Deliverables

Content Creation: Creating and strategizing high-quality, SEO-optimized content for both Logical Media Group and our external clients target audiences. Responsible for writing pieces of optimized keyword-rich SEO content based on variety of industries, topics, trends, and valuable industry updates which should be tailored to search engines and provide high value to targeted audiences.

Keyword research: Use keyword research and content strategies for targeting and ranking for high-volume keywords.

Optimization: Post optimized articles and/or optimized content following industry standards to our CMS platform, social channels and more. Optimize current copy, landing pages and metadata for maximum organic exposure and conversions while maintaining engaging targeted content.

Edit: Provide oversight of SEO, Paid Media, and Email Marketing content written by team members by editing and optimizing per SEO best practices.

Digital Marketing Content Strategies: Craft SEO, Paid Media, and Email Marketing Content strategies and provide proactive recommendations for clients tailored to their unique goals, strengths, and challenges. Prioritize initiatives by impact and ease of implementation.

Cross-Team Communication and Strategy: Work cross-functionally with web, paid, and creative teams to ensure on-time delivery of initiatives and cohesive digital strategy.

Additional Responsibilities

Continued learning: Keep up-to-date with and proactively research news and trends in digital marketing, content, analytics, trending topics, etc. and share with the team.

Team training: Help train Content support staff on basic management initiatives and setup tasks.

Requirements

Basic knowledge of SEO, Paid Media, and Email Marketing and how it applies to Content marketing

Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMRush

Expertise in creative writing incorporating SEO best practices, critical analysis, and editing skills

Understanding of and experience in local search optimization

Understanding of basic HTML as it relates to SEO

Ability to implement foundational SEO recommendations in a Wordpress environment.

Ability to apply best practices and content strategy on various social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, Youtube, etc.)

Excellent analytical, organizational, project management and time management skills

Attention to detail and ability to effectively manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously

Bachelor’s Degree in relevant fields such as english, journalism, Communication, Marketing, etc.

At least 3 years of experience in writing for online publications

Benefits

Option to work completely remote

Logical is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strongly encourage candidates of all different backgrounds and identities to apply. Each new hire is an opportunity for us to bring in a different perspective, and we are always eager to further diversify our company.

#J-18808-Ljbffr Logical Media Group
evaluation_reason: The role is a poor fit primarily due to the 'Remote in US' constraint, which conflicts with your British time zone and location needs. Additionally, the job's heavy emphasis on general SEO content across diverse industries doesn't align with your specialisms in launch/funnel strategy for wellness/personal development brands or your aspiration for high-intellect publications.

job_description:
Position Overview

We're looking for a skilled and versatile Copywriter who can craft high-converting email and landing page copy that drives measurable results for our eCommerce clients. In this role, you'll transform brand strategy into compelling messaging that engages diverse audiences across the customer journey. You’ll play a key part in shaping how our clients communicate, convert, and scale - bringing creative ideas to life in collaboration with designers, strategists, and our broader performance team.

Requirements/Experience:
• 2+ years of copywriting experience, preferably within an agency or eCommerce-focused environment
• Proven portfolio showcasing DTC email campaigns and/or direct-response landing pages
• Familiarity with Klaviyo or other ESPs and an understanding of how email contributes to the overall customer lifecycle
• Solid understanding of core eCommerce metrics such as open rate, click-through rate, conversion rate, and revenue per send
• Ability to write in multiple brand voices and tailor messaging to specific audience segments
• Strong grasp of lifecycle marketing, segmentation, and personalized communication strategies
• Experience collaborating with designers, strategists, and other creatives to bring campaigns from concept to execution
• Exceptional grammar, editing, and proofreading skills with strong attention to detail
• Ability to use AI tools to support research, ideation, and copy output
• Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

Roles & Responsibilities for this position:
• Write high-converting email campaigns and automation flows, ensuring each piece aligns with brand strategy, customer journey stages, and revenue goals
• Develop persuasive landing page copy that clearly communicates product value, reinforces brand positioning, and maximizes conversion rate
• Translate creative direction and strategic briefs into messaging frameworks, subject lines, CTAs, and content outlines that support performance goals
• Adapt copy across a range of tones and audiences, maintaining consistency with each client’s brand voice and overarching messaging strategy
• work closely with designers to ensure copy and visuals work cohesively across email layouts, landing pages, and promotional assets
• Partner with strategists to review performance insights and adjust messaging based on engagement data, testing outcomes, and customer segmentation
• Apply lifecycle marketing principles to craft personalized messaging tailored to subscriber segments, buying behaviors, and customer retention goals
• Review, edit, and refine copy to ensure clarity, accuracy, and alignment with brand standards and compliance requirements
• Leverage AI tools and workflows to streamline research, ideation, and draft development without compromising creativity or quality
• Maintain an organized workflow to manage multiple deadlines, revisions, and campaign calendars across several client accounts

If you’re a performance-driven copywriter who loves turning strategy into results, this role is for you. You’ll collaborate with a talented team, work with growing eCommerce brands, and see your copy directly impact revenue.

Apply today to join our team!

Note: Candidates must be available to work Central Standard Time (CST) business hours.

Pay: $2,000.00 - $4,000.00 per month

Work Location: Remote
evaluation_reason: While your core copywriting and funnel strategy skills align well with the job's focus on high-converting email and landing page copy for eCommerce, the explicit 'US based' requirement directly conflicts with your remote British Time Zone constraint and location in Portugal. Additionally, the role's emphasis on direct-response conversion for eCommerce clients does not align with your aspiration to write for high-intellect/lifestyle publications.

job_description:
About The Company ICF Next is a leading global advisory and technology services provider dedicated to solving complex challenges across various sectors including energy, public health, climate, and more. Our organization combines strategic thinking, creativity, data analytics, and cutting-edge technology to develop innovative solutions that help organizations engage their audiences effectively and drive meaningful action.

We are committed to delivering integrated campaigns and experiences that are not only compelling but also consistent across multiple channels. Our work emphasizes clarity, utility, and performance, ensuring that our clients’ messages resonate and produce measurable results. At ICF Next, we foster a collaborative environment that values diversity, inclusion, and continuous learning, empowering our employees to share their expertise and grow professionally.

About The Role
This role is contingent upon contract award and is not an immediate opening. We are actively conducting interviews and may extend offers in anticipation of the contract finalization, with start dates dependent on the finalization process.

The Junior

Copywriter will play a vital role in developing compelling ideas and translating them into impactful copy across various formats and channels. This position requires a creative thinker who understands the importance of strategic storytelling and can collaborate closely with art directors and other team members from the inception of ideas through execution. The ideal candidate will have a keen eye for detail, a passion for craft, and the ability to craft clear, engaging, and effective messages that align with client objectives. The role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to integrated campaigns that make a difference in public health, energy, climate, and other critical sectors, working within a dynamic and supportive team environment.
Qualifications

• 3+ years of experience in copywriting or content creation

• Strong ability to develop ideas and concepts beyond just writing copy

• Proficiency in creating content across multiple formats including headlines, scripts, and systems

• Experience partnering with art directors and designers to shape creative ideas from inception

• Understanding of how campaigns operate across various channels and moments

• Exceptional language instincts, storytelling skills, and structural awareness

• Curiosity about people, culture, and current trends

• High standards for craft, attention to detail, and a commitment to quality

• Ability to take feedback constructively and push work further

• Strong presentation skills and the ability to articulate ideas clearly

Responsibilities

• Develop innovative ideas and concepts that serve as the foundation for integrated campaigns

• Translate insights into compelling, scalable content across multiple channels and formats

• Write and edit copy for a variety of deliverables including headlines, scripts, digital content, and systems

• Collaborate closely with art directors, strategists, and other team members to shape and refine creative concepts

• Build and maintain a consistent voice and tone that aligns with client brand guidelines and campaign objectives

• Present ideas and work-in-progress to team members and clients, incorporating feedback effectively

• Ensure the quality, clarity, and effectiveness of all written content

• Stay informed about cultural trends and audience preferences to inform creative development

• Contribute to a collaborative team environment that encourages innovation and continuous improvement

Benefits

• Flexible work arrangements, including remote and hybrid options

• Competitive healthcare and retirement plans

• Ongoing professional development and training opportunities

• Collaborative and inclusive work environment

• Opportunities to contribute to impactful social and community initiatives

• Work with a diverse team of professionals committed to innovation and excellence

Equal Opportunity
ICF is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.
We also provide reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, veterans, and individuals with sincerely held religious beliefs throughout the application and employment process.
evaluation_reason: The 'Copywriter, Jr.' role is a significant mismatch for Natalie's 13+ years of experience, and the job's focus on public health, energy, and climate sectors does not align with her niche in wellness and personal development.

job_title: Senior Copywriter / Junior Video Producer (Remote, 1099 Contract, $25-30/hr)
job_description:
Senior Copywriter / Junior Video Producer — Bit House Digital (Remote, 1099 Contractor)

We're a digital marketing agency that runs paid ads for contractors (bathroom, kitchen, tile, decking, fencing, roofing). Our work books their calendars with qualified homeowners. We're growing fast and need a senior copywriter who can also do basic video editing to own our creative refresh loop.

What you'll do:

• Write ad scripts and hooks for our contractor clients (talking-head + b-roll voiceover formats)

• Operate ElevenLabs for voiceovers (we use AI text to voice over for almost every ad)

• Cut clean audio files for our video editor

• Do basic video edits yourself (hook swaps, Canva image ads, testimonial overlays) for clients

• Brief our video editor (we'll guide you on the workflow)

• Maintain our tracking sheet, propose tier changes weekly based on performance

Who you are:

• 2-3+ years of copywriting experience (paid social, lead-gen, or direct response preferred)

• Strong with diagnostic, problem-aware ad copy, you naturally write the way US small business owners talk, not the way ChatGPT writes

• Comfortable with Claude / ChatGPT as a tool, but you edit hard, no AI slop in finished work

• Basic video editing chops (CapCut, Premiere, or similar), we'll teach you what we expect

• Self-directed, you can run a weekly batch without daily check-ins after the first month

• Meta ads experience is a plus

• Open to international: Philippines, South Africa, Argentina, Eastern Europe all welcome. Must have perfect written English and the ability to write US-conversational copy (mid-American voice, not formal or translated English)

Meta policy basics (you'll need these):

You'll be writing Meta ads. There are 3 things that can get a client's ad account banned.
Memorize these:

• Never use the word "financing." Meta flags it as Special Ad Category, which kills targeting. Use "$0 down" or "no payments for 12 months" instead, same offer, different framing.

• Never address negative personal attributes. Bad: "are you tired of being broke?" or "Your house looks outdated." Good: "Most kitchens in [city] are 20+ years overdue."

• Never frame home services as health/safety. Bad: "Your old kitchen is making your family sick." Good: "Your old kitchen is costing you time and stress."

Everything else in Meta's policy book we'll teach you. These 3 are the deal-breakers.

What you’ll need:

• Reliable laptop or desktop (Mac or Windows, 8GB RAM minimum)

• Stable internet (minimum 25 Mbps down / 10 Mbps up)

• Video editing tool: we provide a CapCut Pro seat. Premiere or Final Cut preferred if you already have one (we don't reimburse personal software).

• Telegram account (daily team comms)

• Toggl account (free, for tracking hours)

• Comfortable with Claude or ChatGPT as a writing tool. We'll add you to our Claude Team workspace with all our custom prompts and reference materials in week 1.

Compensation:

• 1099 contractor, $25-$30/hr starting depending on experience

• Path to full time as the agency grows (we're scaling fast)

• 15-20 hr/week during 2-week paid trial, 20-25 hr/week ongoing

• Weekly invoicing via Wise / Rippling

Why work with us:

• Fully remote, work from anywhere

• Flexible hours, as long as you hit your weekly deliverables and the Monday standup, work when you work best

• No client meetings, no client emails, you write the copy, we handle client comms. Pure craft, no account management drama.

• Real performance data on every ad you write (CPL, CTR, CVR). Portfolio gold. Most copywriting jobs never let you see what actually performed.

• We pay for the tools (Claude Team, ElevenLabs, Canva Pro, CapCut Pro). You don't pay out of pocket.

• Direct access to the founder, small team, your ideas get heard the same week, no corporate layers.

• Part-time now, full-time later. We're scaling to 15-20 client accounts and this role grows with us. Strong performers move to full-time with raises and bonuses.

To apply (IMPORTANT):

Write a 3-hook + 30-second talking-head script for the dummy client below. Submit it with your application. No cover letter needed, your copy IS your cover letter. Take no more than 15 minutes on this.

DUMMY CLIENT — Lumina Remodeling

- Seattle, WA. High-end bathroom remodeling. Average project value $40-60k.

- This ad targets US homeowners (Seattle area). Script must sound like a real person talking to a real US homeowner, casual, conversational, mid-American voice. Not formal English, not translated English, not "elevate your bathing experience" English.

Current offer to feature: Free heated floors with any full bath remodel (limited-time)

About the company (use what's relevant, you decide what matters):

• 10-year workmanship warranty on every project

• Custom design process, every project starts with a free 3D design layout before any work begins

• In-house design + build team (no subcontracted labor)

• Designer and dedicated project manager assigned to every job

• Uses premium materials (Kohler, Toto, Brizo)

• Local Seattle showroom open to the public

• 13 years in business

• Family-owned

• 300+ completed projects in the Seattle area

• 4.8-star Google rating with 150+ reviews

• Featured in Seattle Met Magazine (2024 "Best Bathroom Remodelers")

• Founded in 2013 by Mike Johnson, who started his career as a plumber

• Office hours: Monday-Friday 9-5

• $0 down financing available, no payments for 12 months on new bathroom projects

• Owns a fleet of 2 branded service vans

• Recently donated $5,000 to Seattle Elementary's art program

• Has 8 full-time employees

Write:

• 3 unique high-intent hooks, must be problem-specific diagnostic questions

• One 30-second talking-head or b-roll voiceover base script that flows naturally from any of the 3 hooks

Hard requirements:

• Limited-time urgency built into the script

• The free heated floors offer lands in the FIRST HALF of the script (not the end)

• Each hook transitions cleanly into the base script (no jarring shift)

• US contractor voice, conversational, no AI tells, no em dashes

• Full base script + one hook must read aloud in 45 seconds or less.

Take no more than 15 minutes on this. We're looking at how you write and what you choose to include, not how much you can pack in.

Send your script + a quick note about your experience to valsvity@gmail.com. We review applications on a rolling basis and reply within 5 business days to candidates we want to interview.

Pay: $25.00 - $30.00 per hour

Benefits:

• Flexible schedule

Application Question(s):

• Paste your 3 hooks + 30-second script below

Work Location: Remote
evaluation_reason: While the role aligns with direct response copywriting and remote work, there is a significant mismatch in content niche, desired aesthetic, strategic scope (tactical vs. strategic), and likely compensation for Natalie's extensive experience and specific needs.

job_description:
The Senior Brand Copywriter is a subject matter expert in brand marketing copywriting, producing sharp and unexpected copy designed to engage, inform and convert our B2C and B2B audiences, including current and prospective members, dentists, groups, brokers and corporate audiences across all channels. This includes full-scale integrated marketing campaigns complete with social media, email, print, video, UX, out of home, direct, and other multi-media communications.

This Brand & Creative team role supports Marketing, Sales, People, Communications and other internal stakeholders. It requires solid marketing writing skills and demonstrated expertise in developing snappy taglines, short action-driven email copy, memorable video scripts, product and service names and social media copy. It also requires experience in how content strategy informs copy and a knack for translating business objectives and strategy into engaging campaign concepts in collaboration with creative partners and business stakeholders. The Senior Brand Copywriter masters the art of the pitch, presenting creative work to internal stakeholders with clarity and confidence. They mentor junior writers, lead campaign ideation sprints and edit and polish copy concepts for presentation to creative leadership. Their copywriting reflects the brand tone and voice, messaging and content strategy and delivers memorable, audience-specific content that elevates the Delta Dental brand.

Behind the smile! We are dedicated to safeguarding the health and financial stability of our employees and their loved ones. This commitment extends beyond the workplace to foster personal growth and holistic wellbeing. Our life-changing rewards package includes:

• Competitive base and incentive pay

• 401(k) with robust matching and non-matching contributions

• Rich medical & pharmacy benefits

• 100% employer-paid dental and vision benefits

• Holistic wellbeing program with deep financial incentives

• Generous paid time off plus 12 paid holidays and your birthday off

• Culture of growth and learning: career development; tuition reimbursement; recognition program

• Family support: adoption assistance, fertility treatment, child, elder & pet care assistance

• Social responsibility and volunteer opportunities

• Employee discount program

Fair Chance Ordinances and Criminal Background Considerations

This position requires that the applicant undergo a background check. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with any applicable federal, state or local laws, including, but not limited to, the California Fair Chance Act, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and/or New York Corrections Law Article 23-A.

Important Work Authorization Information

Please note, Delta Dental will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position.

#LI-Remote

At Delta Dental, we’re behind millions of smiles and counting. For over 68 years, we’ve served our communities by increasing access to affordable, quality care – but that commitment to care extends beyond the people we serve, to the dedicated employees who make those smiles genuine. We've come this far by building fair programs that offer opportunities for advancement, create meaningful connections and improve the lives of our employees and their families. Because we know that when we take exceptional care of each other, we can make a positive impact for all.

We champion an inspirational workplace through our commitment to trust, service, excellence and innovation. Joining us means working for a company that offers stability and balance, opportunities for leadership at all levels, and a work environment focused on teamwork and camaraderie.

Discover the smiling faces behind Delta Dental and experience our values and culture in action by connecting with us on social media. Follow Delta Dental Ins. on Facebook and Instagram, #LifeatDDins on Facebook and Instagram, Delta Dental Ins. on Twitter and Delta Dental Ins. on LinkedIn. If you've got an appetite for innovation and want to be a part of a team that’s transforming the future of health care, join us!

Since 1955, we have offered comprehensive, high-quality oral health care benefits to millions of enrollees and built the strongest network of dental providers in the country. The Delta Dental of California network includes affiliates Delta Dental Insurance Company; Delta Dental of Pennsylvania; Delta Dental of New York, Inc.; Delta Dental of the District of Columbia; Delta Dental of Delaware, Inc.; and Delta Dental of West Virginia, providing dental benefits to more than 31 million people across 15 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands.

The company and its affiliates are part of Delta Dental Plans Association (DDPA), a not-for-profit national association based in Oak Brook, Illinois. Through our national network of Delta Dental companies, we offer dental coverage in all 50 states, Puerto Rico and other U.S. territories. We offer vision coverage through DeltaVision in 15 states and the District of Columbia. Collectively, we deliver benefits to more Americans than any other dental insurance company.

Delta Dental Ins. is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination and harassment. Individuals seeking employment at Delta Dental are considered without regard to race, ethnicity, color, age, sex, religion, national origin, ancestry, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information, actual or perceived physical or mental disability, registered domestic partner status, caregiver status, marital status, veteran or military status, citizenship status, or any other legally protected category. Delta Dental is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment. Accordingly, we have adopted and maintain an Equal Employment Opportunity Policy, Harassment-Free Work Environment Policy, and Respectful Workplace policies and will not tolerate violations of these policies.

Unfortunately, our Delta Dental Enterprise is unable to hire individuals residing in Alaska, North Dakota, Nebraska, Hawaii, Oklahoma, Vermont, Maine, West Virginia, New Hampshire, Wyoming, Puerto Rico, District of Columbia (Washington D.C.) or other US Territories at this time.

job_description:
Team Rubicon (TR) is seeking a Leased Employee, Copywriter to join our Brand & Communications team! The Leased Employee, Copywriter will develop compelling, on-brand copy across digital and print channels, support campaign messaging, and help bring Team Rubicon’s voice to life across brand & comms initiatives. This role requires strong writing and editing skills, creativity, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate across teams in a fast-paced environment.

Every day will be a challenge, but every month brings new opportunities for an organization that is quickly becoming a household name in disaster response. This position reports to TR’s Director, Brand and is based remote anywhere within the United States.

This is a temporary assignment lasting 6-months and will be managed through a third-party staffing agency partnered with Team Rubicon.

Project: TR BrandComms Copywriting Support

Duration: Immediate – November 2026

Expected Hours Per Week: 10-15

Duties:

• Draft clear, compelling copy across various Team Rubicon channels, including digital and print advertising, video scripts, social media, email, web, and print collateral.

• Partner with the Director of Brand and cross-functional members of the Brand & Communications (BrandComms) team to support creative concepting and message development.

• Provide copyediting and proofreading support to ensure accuracy, consistency, and adherence to Team Rubicon’s brand voice and editorial standards.

Education and Background:

• 4-7 years of professional copywriting experience ideally within a mission-driven space

• Bachelor’s degree in Journalism, English, Communications, Public Relations, or Creative Writing

• Experience working on a multidisciplinary creative team, such as an in-house Creative or Marketing team preferred

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to adapt tone and style for a variety of audiences

• Demonstrated ability to concept and write across a range of formats and channels

Special Requirements:

• This is a contingent; REMOTE position and can be based anywhere within the United States and provides a flexible & non-traditional work schedule

Job Type:

• Contingent; hourly, non-exempt

Pay Range: 

• $28.00 - $38.00 per hour

The hiring range reflects where in the range we most likely intend to hire for this role. Additionally, compensation within that range can vary for several reasons including market conditions, cost of living, skills/capabilities, experience, etc.

Cultural Values: 

• Mission First, Greyshirts Always: Anyone joining TR must understand that our mission to provide disaster response comes first

• Step Into The Arena: TR needs leaders who aren’t afraid to dare to be great 

• Everyone Has A Role Know It: Ability to successfully navigate a fast-paced, high-growth environment and solve problems in the face of ambiguity 

• GSD: We are entrepreneurial, resourceful, and determined no matter how chaotic the situation 

• Change Your Socks: We take care of ourselves and each other so we are best equipped to serve those in greatest need 

• Adults Only: Every team member is an adult until proven otherwise 

• Your Mother’s A Donor: Every leader must be committed to fiduciary responsibility, transparency, and financial stewardship 

Learn more about Team Rubicon: 

• Website: Team Rubicon USA

• LinkedIn: Team Rubicon

• Facebook: Team Rubicon

• X: @TeamRubicon

• YouTube: Team Rubicon

• Instagram: @teamrubicon

• Threads: @teamrubicon

• TikTok: @teamrubicon

Team Rubicon is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to not only including but actively seeking out a diversity of lifestyles, experiences, perspectives, and voices in order to foster inclusion, growth, and innovation. Your candidacy will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
evaluation_reason: The role's content niche (disaster response) and aesthetic fundamentally mismatch your preferred domains (wellness, personal development). job_description:

Location: New York, NY (Remote)

HERE’S WHAT YOU’LL DO

VARITE is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Remote from Latin America

Schedule: Monday - Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM Mountain Time
Job Overview

Our client is a dynamic, fast-growing digital marketing agency that's been rapidly scaling over the past 12 months.

As a young and fun team, they're passionate about delivering exceptional results for
their clients across diverse industries. They're a small, senior-level marketing agency that partners with brands in outdoor adventure, local services (like auto and home), meds pa/health, and e-commerce. They act as a fractional CMO + marketing team for their clients—building strategy, running campaigns, and creating content that actually drives revenue, not just vanity metrics. We're seeking a Content Writer / SEO Specialist who loves writing, can adapt to different brand voices, and understands how search and content work together to grow traffic, leads, and sales.

This is a hands-on writing role with a strong SEO component. You’ll be responsible for planning, writing, and optimizing content across multiple brands and industries. One day you might be outlining a blog post for an adventure tour company, the next you’re reworking service pages for a local dent repair shop, the next you’re writing an email campaign for a med spa. If you’re a creative, high-level English writer who also enjoys keyword research, on-page SEO, and basic analytics—and you like variety—this role is for you.

Responsibilities:
Content & Copywriting
• Own copywriting for the agency and client brands, including:

• Promotional materials: one-pagers, print pieces, simple sales collateral
• Google Business Profile content: business descriptions, services, posts, and product blurbs
SEO & Strategy Support

• Optimize and update existing content to improve rankings, click-through rates, and engagement

• Help build and maintain content calendars based on search demand, brand priorities, and campaign needs
Collaboration & Workflow
• Work closely with our leadership team and designers to bring campaigns and pages to life
• Take constructive feedback well and iterate quickly
• Manage your time and tasks in a project management tool (e.g., ClickUp or similar) and consistently hit deadlines
• Ask smart questions, surface ideas, and contribute to content strategy—not just fulfill
tickets
Qualifications, Skills and Key Competencies
Non-Negotiable Requirements
• Exceptional written English.

• You write clearly, naturally, and persuasively for US audiences. Your grammar, structure, and tone are strong. • Proven content + SEO experience. • At least 4+ years writing marketing content for websites, blogs, and/or email. • Hands-on experience with on-page SEO (not just “I know what keywords are”). • Brand voice chameleon. • You can learn a brand quickly and adjust your writing style across very different industries and tones. • Comfort with SEO tools & analytics. • Able to perform basic keyword research and build outlines based on that research.

• Comfortable reading Google Analytics / GA4 and Google Search Console at a basic level. You don’t have to be a data analyst, but you do need to be able to pull insights and make content decisions. • Channel versatility. • Experience writing for websites, blogs, email, and at least some short-form promo copy (SMS, ads, etc.). • Reliable, organized, and proactive. • You manage your own time well, meet deadlines, and communicate early if something might slip. • You’re curious, coachable, and open to feedback.

Nice-to-Have (Not Required, But a Plus)
• Experience working with US agencies or international clients
• Familiarity with WordPre

job_description:
*This role is open to remote or hybrid candidates (East Coast preference), with hybrid being central to our New York, NY or Charlotte area offices.

We're looking for a Senior Marketing Copywriter to own the words behind Bankrate's marketing channels — from paid social and display to email, landing pages, and direct mail. This is a high-output, direct response role for someone who writes with intention, tests with rigor, and measures success in clicks, conversions, and revenue. You'll be embedded in the marketing team, working directly with performance and lifecycle managers to translate strategy into copy that moves people to act. You'll help shape the testing agenda, bring your own hypotheses, and use performance data to make your next draft better than your last.

What You’ll Do:

• Paid social and display copy: Write headlines, hooks, body copy, and CTAs for ads across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, programmatic display, and streaming/CTV. Build variants designed for testing, with clear differences in angle, tone, and message that generate real signal.

• Creative testing: Partner with performance managers to develop copy-driven test hypotheses. Know what lever you're pulling with each variant and document learnings so they compound across campaigns.

• Volume with craft: Maintain high output without defaulting to generic copy. The tenth variant should be as considered as the first.

• Email copy: Write subject lines, preheaders, and body copy for trigger-based and broadcast campaigns. Support A/B testing across angles, CTAs, and length. Understand how copy interacts with layout to drive opens and clicks.

• CRO-focused page copy: Write and iterate on landing page copy built for conversion. Translate test briefs into distinct treatments — headline, subhead, body, CTA — and use performance data to sharpen each iteration.

• Message hierarchy: Structure copy to guide attention and reduce friction. Know what to lead with, what to support, and what to cut.

• Direct mail copy: Write copy for direct mail campaigns — envelope teaser, letter copy, inserts, and response devices. Understand the read patterns and attention dynamics of physical mail and write to them.

• Compliance-aware writing: Write effective direct response copy within financial services compliance constraints. Work with legal review processes, incorporate required disclosures without killing conversion, and find the persuasive angle that still clears the bar.

What We’re Looking For:

• Direct response track record: Experience writing DR copy with accountability to performance metrics. You've seen the data on your own copy and used it to improve the next round. Portfolio shows breadth across paid ads, email, and landing pages.

• Multi-channel fluency: You've written across paid social, email, landing pages, and direct mail and understand the format constraints and conversion dynamics of each.

• Hypothesis-driven tester: You develop a point of view on what should win and why, track results, and build a compounding body of copy knowledge — not just a portfolio of variants.

• Compliance-fluent: You have experience writing in regulated industries. You know how to advocate for effective copy within compliance constraints, not just around them.

• Collaborative and fast: You work well with designers and channel managers, can push back when strategy is unclear, and ship strong work without waiting for perfect inputs.

• Structured and self-directed: You manage multiple workstreams, flag blockers early, and deliver without heavy oversight.

• AI-forward practitioner: You use AI tools to accelerate drafting and iteration. You evaluate outputs critically for voice, accuracy, and compliance, and know where they add real leverage.

Compensation:

This range reflects total cash compensation, which may include base salary only or base salary plus target bonus, depending on the role. Where eligible, equity may also be offered separately and not included below. Actual compensation varies based on location, experience, and qualifications.

• Total Cash Compensation Range: $90,000 – $130,000 per year

Additionally, the following benefits are provided by Red Ventures, subject to eligibility requirements.

• Health Insurance Coverage (medical, dental, and vision)

• Life Insurance

• Short and Long-Term Disability Insurance

• Flexible Spending Accounts

• Holiday Pay

• 401(k) with match

• Employee Assistance Program

• Paid Parental Bonding Benefit Program

• Flexible Paid Time Off (PTO): We believe time to rest and recharge is essential. That’s why we offer a generous and flexible PTO policy. Full-time employees accrue 20 days of PTO for a full calendar year annually, with an increase to 25 days after five years of service.

Who We Are:

Bankrate is redefining the future of financial decision-making. For nearly five decades, we’ve empowered tens of millions of consumers to make smarter financial choices and helped hundreds of leading financial institutions grow. Today, we’re evolving from a marketplace into a next-generation technology platform—underpinned by proprietary data, AI-driven innovation, and deep enterprise integrations. With our track record of delivering consumers quantifiably better offers—for example, on home loans where we lead the market—Bankrate stands at the center of the $60B U.S. financial services acquisition opportunity. As we expand through omnichannel marketing and strategic partnerships, and launch unique consumer product applications, we’re building a more efficient, personalized, and connected financial ecosystem—one grounded in trust. Join us as we transform five decades of credibility into the next era of financial technology.

Red Ventures is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or any other basis protected by law. Employment at Red Ventures is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications.

We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to qualified individuals with disabilities. This includes providing reasonable accommodation where appropriate. Should you require a reasonable accommodation to apply or participate in the job application or interview process, please contact accommodation@redventures.com.

If you are based in California, we encourage you to read this important information for California residents linked here.

At Red Ventures, we believe in real human connection. That’s why we do not hire someone through text, social media, or email only. As part of the hiring process, you should expect live conversations with RV teammates before any offer is made. Also, keep an eye on the sender: we only use official @redventures.com email addresses at the portfolio level or business specific email addresses (e.g., @thepointsguy.com), not ones like “redventurescareer.com.” We will never ask candidates to send money, buy equipment, or share financial account info during your journey with us. You can always find our open roles on redventures.com— if you receive a message that seems suspicious, please use redventures.com to verify the opportunity.

For more, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission has published helpful articles to help individuals learn more about protecting themselves from recruiter scams. If you think you’ve been targeted, feel free to report it to your local authorities. Stay safe out there!

job_description:
Senior Copywriter / Junior Video Producer — Bit House Digital (Remote, 1099 Contractor)

We're a digital marketing agency that runs paid ads for contractors (bathroom, kitchen, tile, decking, fencing, roofing). Our work books their calendars with qualified homeowners. We're growing fast and need a senior copywriter who can also do basic video editing to own our creative refresh loop.

What you'll do:
• Write ad scripts and hooks for our contractor clients (talking-head + b-roll voiceover formats)
• Operate ElevenLabs for voiceovers (we use AI text to voice over for almost every ad)
• Cut clean audio files for our video editor
• Do basic video edits yourself (hook swaps, Canva image ads, testimonial overlays) for clients
• Brief our video editor (we'll guide you on the workflow)
• Maintain our tracking sheet, propose tier changes weekly based on performance

Who you are:
• 2-3+ years of copywriting experience (paid social, lead-gen, or direct response preferred)
• Strong with diagnostic, problem-aware ad copy, you naturally write the way US small business owners talk, not the way ChatGPT writes
• Comfortable with Claude / ChatGPT as a tool, but you edit hard, no AI slop in finished work
• Basic video editing chops (CapCut, Premiere, or similar), we'll teach you what we expect
• Self-directed, you can run a weekly batch without daily check-ins after the first month
• Meta ads experience is a plus
• Open to international: Philippines, South Africa, Argentina, Eastern Europe all welcome. Must have perfect written English and the ability to write US-conversational copy (mid-American voice, not formal or translated English)

Meta policy basics (you'll need these):

You'll be writing Meta ads. There are 3 things that can get a client's ad account banned.
Memorize these:
• Never use the word "financing." Meta flags it as Special Ad Category, which kills targeting. Use "$0 down" or "no payments for 12 months" instead, same offer, different framing.
• Never address negative personal attributes. Bad: "are you tired of being broke?" or "Your house looks outdated." Good: "Most kitchens in [city] are 20+ years overdue."
• Never frame home services as health/safety. Bad: "Your old kitchen is making your family sick." Good: "Your old kitchen is costing you time and stress."

Everything else in Meta's policy book we'll teach you. These 3 are the deal-breakers.

What you’ll need:
• Reliable laptop or desktop (Mac or Windows, 8GB RAM minimum)
• Stable internet (minimum 25 Mbps down / 10 Mbps up)
• Video editing tool: we provide a CapCut Pro seat. Premiere or Final Cut preferred if you already have one (we don't reimburse personal software).
• Telegram account (daily team comms)
• Toggl account (free, for tracking hours)
• Comfortable with Claude or ChatGPT as a writing tool. We'll add you to our Claude Team workspace with all our custom prompts and reference materials in week 1.

Compensation:
• 1099 contractor, $25-$30/hr starting depending on experience
• Path to full time as the agency grows (we're scaling fast)
• 15-20 hr/week during 2-week paid trial, 20-25 hr/week ongoing
• Weekly invoicing via Wise / Rippling

Why work with us:
• Fully remote, work from anywhere
• Flexible hours, as long as you hit your weekly deliverables and the Monday standup, work when you work best
• No client meetings, no client emails, you write the copy, we handle client comms. Pure craft, no account management drama.
• Real performance data on every ad you write (CPL, CTR, CVR). Portfolio gold. Most copywriting jobs never let you see what actually performed.
• We pay for the tools (Claude Team, ElevenLabs, Canva Pro, CapCut Pro). You don't pay out of pocket.
• Direct access to the founder, small team, your ideas get heard the same week, no corporate layers.
• Part-time now, full-time later. We're scaling to 15-20 client accounts and this role grows with us. Strong performers move to full-time with raises and bonuses.

To apply (IMPORTANT):

Write a 3-hook + 30-second talking-head script for the dummy client below. Submit it with your application. No cover letter needed, your copy IS your cover letter. Take no more than 15 minutes on this.

DUMMY CLIENT — Lumina Remodeling

- Seattle, WA. High-end bathroom remodeling. Average project value $40-60k.

- This ad targets US homeowners (Seattle area). Script must sound like a real person talking to a real US homeowner, casual, conversational, mid-American voice. Not formal English, not translated English, not "elevate your bathing experience" English.

Current offer to feature: Free heated floors with any full bath remodel (limited-time)

About the company (use what's relevant, you decide what matters):
• 10-year workmanship warranty on every project
• Custom design process, every project starts with a free 3D design layout before any work begins
• In-house design + build team (no subcontracted labor)
• Designer and dedicated project manager assigned to every job
• Uses premium materials (Kohler, Toto, Brizo)
• Local Seattle showroom open to the public
• 13 years in business
• Family-owned
• 300+ completed projects in the Seattle area
• 4.8-star Google rating with 150+ reviews
• Featured in Seattle Met Magazine (2024 "Best Bathroom Remodelers")
• Founded in 2013 by Mike Johnson, who started his career as a plumber
• Office hours: Monday-Friday 9-5
• $0 down financing available, no payments for 12 months on new bathroom projects
• Owns a fleet of 2 branded service vans
• Recently donated $5,000 to Seattle Elementary's art program
• Has 8 full-time employees

Write:
• 3 unique high-intent hooks, must be problem-specific diagnostic questions
• One 30-second talking-head or b-roll voiceover base script that flows naturally from any of the 3 hooks

Hard requirements:
• Limited-time urgency built into the script
• The free heated floors offer lands in the FIRST HALF of the script (not the end)
• Each hook transitions cleanly into the base script (no jarring shift)
• US contractor voice, conversational, no AI tells, no em dashes
• Full base script + one hook must read aloud in 45 seconds or less.

Take no more than 15 minutes on this. We're looking at how you write and what you choose to include, not how much you can pack in.

Send your script + a quick note about your experience to valsvity@gmail.com. We review applications on a rolling basis and reply within 5 business days to candidates we want to interview.

Pay: $25.00 - $30.00 per hour

Benefits:
• Flexible schedule

Application Question(s):
• Paste your 3 hooks + 30-second script below

Work Location: Remote
evaluation_reason: Despite strong copywriting skills, the role's niche (contractor services) and required aesthetic ('US small business owners talk') fundamentally clash with Natalie's preference for wellness/personal development brands and high-intellect publications. Furthermore, the job does not align with the critical British time zone constraint.

job_description:
About the Team

DoorDash's mission is to grow and empower local economies. By building intelligent, last-mile delivery technology for local cities, DoorDash connects people with the local businesses they care about - helping grow businesses and the communities that support those businesses. To further this mission, DoorDash is building a best in class Creative Studio. We will be populated by diverse, eclectic talent. We take risks, challenge each other, and inspire new ways of thinking. We're here to create work that we, our partners, and the entire DoorDash community can be immensely proud of. We're here to make the best work of our careers

About the Role

We're looking for an experienced and versatile Editorial Copywriter to join the creative team in supporting marketing efforts across all DoorDash audiences: Consumer, Merchant, and Dasher. You'll help push the DoorDash brand forward with outstanding copywriting for ad campaigns, social media, in-app content, our website, video, email and more. Reporting to the Editorial Lead, you'll work closely with art directors, designers, and marketing partners to tell the most impactful brand and product stories. You're organized and detail-obsessed, and you can prove that remarkable communications don't require a lot of time. You'll be an ambassador of our brand voice and join a team that's defining editorial excellence for DoorDash.

You're excited about this opportunity because you will…

• Write compelling copy for marketing materials including ad campaigns, social media, in-app content, our website, video, email and more
• Work closely and collaboratively with art directors and partners to deliver on strategic projects
• Contribute to creative ideation and campaign development for brand and product, and be an ambassador for the DoorDash voice
• Translate technical business challenges into relatable human narratives
• Masterfully tailor your communications to different audiences, channels, and situations
• Write, write, write! You will be all about execution within a fast-paced and agile team.
• Represent the writer's perspective to team members, both on the page and in presentations

We're excited about you because…

• 5+ years of creative, editorial, and/or product writing experience at an agency or in-house
• A compelling portfolio that proves you can write snappy headlines, clear informational copy, and phrases that delight
• The ability to multitask, ask strategic questions, and prioritize with ease
• Experience managing your portion of the workload across multiple projects with tight timelines
• A firm grasp on content strategy and how to structure information to achieve our shared goals
• An eye for design and an understanding of how art and copy work together
• A strong work ethic, an inquisitive mind, and an eagerness to be proactive

Why You'll Love Working at DoorDash

• We are leaders – Leadership is not limited to our management team. It's something everyone at DoorDash embraces and embodies.
• We are operators – We believe the only way to predict the future is to build it. Creating solutions to lead our company and our industry is what we do on every project, every day.
• We are learners – Everyone here is continually learning on the job, no matter if we've been in a role for one year or one minute. We are committed to learning and implementing what is best for our customers, merchants, and dashers.
• We are one team – The magic of DoorDash is our people, together making our inspiring goals attainable and driving us to greater heights.
• We offer great compensation packages and comprehensive health benefits.
• This job is remote friendly.

About DoorDash

At DoorDash, our mission to empower local economies shapes how our team members move quickly and always learn and reiterate to support merchants, Dashers and the communities we serve. We are a technology and logistics company that started with door-to-door delivery, and we are looking for team members who can help us go from a company that is known for delivering food to a company that people turn to for any and all goods. Read more on the DoorDash website, the DoorDash blog, the DoorDash Engineering blog, and the DoorDash Careers page.

DoorDash is growing rapidly and changing constantly, which gives our team members the opportunity to share their unique perspectives, solve new challenges, and own their careers. Our leaders seek the truth and welcome big, hairy, audacious questions. We are grounded in our company values, and we make intentional decisions that are both logical and display empathy for our range of users—from Dashers to Merchants to Customers.

We're committed to supporting employees' happiness, healthiness, and overall well-being by providing comprehensive benefits and perks including premium healthcare, wellness expense reimbursement, paid parental leave and more.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We're committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That's why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

Statement of Non-Discrimination: In keeping with our beliefs and goals, no employee or applicant will face discrimination or harassment based on: race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, age, gender, marital/domestic partner status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability status, or veteran status. Above and beyond discrimination and harassment based on "protected categories," we also aim to prevent other subtler forms of inappropriate behavior (i.e., stereotyping) from ever gaining a foothold in our office. Whether blatant or hidden, barriers to success have no place at DoorDash. We value a diverse workforce – people who identify as women, non-binary or gender non-conforming, LGBTQIA+, American Indian or Native Alaskan, Black or African American, Hispanic or Latinx, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, differently-abled, caretakers and parents, and veterans are encouraged to apply. Thank you to the Level Playing Field Institute for this statement of non-discrimination.

If you need any accommodations, please inform your recruiting contact upon initial connection.
evaluation_reason: While Natalie possesses the core copywriting skills required, the role's content niche (DoorDash vs. wellness/personal development), aesthetic alignment, and critical remote/British time zone constraints are all significantly mismatched.

job_title: Brand Copywriter | Remote | Open Across US
job_description:
About Comrade Digital Marketing

We are a mid-sized marketing company established in 2008 and headquartered in Chicago. We provide search marketing services, namely SEO, GEO, PPC, web design and development. And we do it well!

We have an amazing team with very low turnover because we all love it here. There are many reasons why we love working here, but most importantly:

• We love the people we work with
• We are always growing professionally, individually, and as a team
• Our management team is great (respectful, fair, and flexible when needed)
• Our work is interesting, dynamic, and not mired in managerial bureaucracy
• Our compensation is strong
• Lastly, because our team is not huge, each of us creates a real impact on our clients and everyone on the team.

This job is open across the US.

About The Role

As our Brand Copywriter, you will be the dedicated writer for brand-focused content across the Comrade and Grow Law websites. You will be responsible for producing high-quality content across a variety of formats, including website copy, blogs, landing pages, and other digital marketing assets.

This is a high-output role for someone who can balance creativity with structure, move quickly without sacrificing quality, and write with both the audience and search performance in mind.

You will report directly to the Content Manager and collaborate regularly with the content team to ensure consistency in messaging, tone, and execution.

What You'll Do

• Write and edit a high volume of marketing content for the Comrade and Grow Law websites
• Create engaging copy for web pages, blog articles, landing pages, and other brand content
• Apply SEO best practices to improve organic visibility and performance
• Collaborate daily with the content team to align on priorities, voice, messaging, and deadlines
• Revise copy based on stakeholder feedback and evolving creative direction
• Proofread and quality-check all content to ensure clarity, accuracy, consistency, and polish
• Help maintain a strong and consistent brand voice across digital touch points

What Success Looks Like

In this role, success means:

• Delivering high-quality content on time and with minimal revisions
• Writing copy that is clear, engaging, and aligned with brand tone
• Supporting SEO goals through well-optimized content
• Managing a consistent writing output across multiple content types
• Responding well to feedback and maintaining strong collaboration with the team

Required Qualifications

What We're Looking For

• 2–5 years of professional copywriting experience in a marketing environment
• Strong writing and editing skills, with the ability to adapt tone and messaging to fit brand voice
• Solid understanding of SEO principles and how they apply to digital content
• Excellent attention to detail and strong time management skills
• Native-level English fluency
• Strong communication skills and a collaborative, feedback-friendly working style

Preferred Qualifications

• Experience working in a marketing agency or in-house brand environment
• Background in journalism or legal-industry content
• Degree in Journalism, English, Communications, or a related field
• Familiarity with tools such as SEMrush, Ahrefs, SurferSEO, Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and AI writing tools

Tools You May Use

• Depending on workflow and project needs, you may work with tools such as:
• Google Docs
• SEO platforms like SEMrush, Ahrefs, or SurferSEO
• Google Analytics and Google Search Console
• AI-assisted writing or research tools
• Internal collaboration and content workflow systems

Work Environment

• Fully remote role
• Full-time position
• Collaborative and feedback-driven team culture
• Opportunity to work on meaningful brand content with visible business impact
evaluation_reason: The role's focus on B2B agency and legal content with an "Open Across US" time zone is a significant mismatch for your niche in transformational brands and British time zone requirement. Your high-level strategic, launch, and direct response skills are also not fully aligned with this brand-focused, SEO-centric content creation role.

job_description:
This assignment posting is for an Agency Worker opportunity with People Inc. The Agency Worker is paid by a staffing agency employer. Agency Workers will receive a W2 from the staffing agency reporting their earnings for tax purposes.
Copywriter, Paid Memberships (Contract)
The Paid Memberships team at People Inc. is looking for a contract Copywriter to support copy on a new paid membership program. In this role, you'll write across the full subscriber lifecycle from acquisition landing pages and paywalls to upgrade prompts, promotional offers, and tier comparisons, creating copy that drives sign-ups, upgrades, and long-term engagement. You'll bring a test-and-learn mindset and sharp editorial instincts to every project. This is a hands-on contract role for a writer who can hit the ground running, meet deadlines, and deliver conversion-focused copy that's also on-brand.
Assignment Details
• Hourly Rate: $55-65/Hour

• Hours: 20 Hours Per Week/Hybrid (1 day in office)

• Location: Remote

• Start Date: ASAP

• End Date: 3 months with the potential to extend

What You’ll Do
• Write clear, compelling, conversion-focused copy across landing pages, paywalls, upgrade prompts, offer pages, and more.

• Write promotional copy for discounts, tier comparisons, and offers that drive sign-ups, upgrades, and renewals.

• Tailor messaging to specific audience segments and lifecycle stages, while also contributing to broad-reach brand and acquisition campaigns.

• Develop and write A/B tests, then refine copy based on performance data to continuously improve conversion and retention.

• Translate complex value propositions, pricing structures, and membership benefits into simple, persuasive language.

• Maintain a consistent brand voice and tone across every subscriber touchpoint.

Experience:
• 3+ years of copywriting experience, with at least 1 year specifically working on subscription products, membership programs, or recurring revenue businesses.

• Proven ability to write across the subscriber lifecycle — acquisition, conversion, and retention.

• Experience with A/B testing, optimizing based on data and writing to specific audience segments in addition to broad-reach messaging.

• Proven track-record of writing discount promos, tier comparisons, and offer copy that converts.

Skills:
• Strong command of persuasive, conversion-focused copy across discount promos, tier comparisons, and offer copy.

• Sharp editorial judgment with attention to voice, tone, and brand consistency.

• Organized and able to manage multiple priorities without sacrificing quality.

• Collaborative, solution-oriented, and comfortable working in a fast-paced team environment

People Inc. is the largest print and digital publisher in America. Nearly 200 million people trust us each month to help them make decisions, take action, and find inspiration. People Inc.'s over 40 iconic brands include PEOPLE, Better Homes & Gardens, Verywell, Food & Wine, Travel + Leisure, Allrecipes, REAL SIMPLE, Investopedia, and Southern Living.
Please be aware of fraudulent recruiters offering opportunities at People Inc. If you are in conversations about a job opportunity and wish to confirm its validity, please reach out directly to hrconcerns@people.inc.

Remote

About the Company:
About.com
evaluation_reason: While Natalie's conversion copywriting skills and experience align well with the job's requirements, the role explicitly states 'Hybrid (1 day in office)' which contradicts the 'Must be a Remote role' constraint. job_title: Copywriter job at Trevor Project in AK, HI
job_description:
Title: Copywriter

Location: Remote - United States

Type: Full Time

Workplace: remote

Category: Content

Job Description:

About Trevor:

The Trevor Project is the leading suicide prevention and crisis intervention organization for LGBTQ+ young people. Trevor offers 24/7 crisis services, connecting highly trained counselors with LGBTQ+ young people whenever they need support. To drive prevention efforts, The Trevor Project also operates robust research, advocacy, education, and peer support programs. We’ve been saving lives every day for over 25 years.

Every day, and in every role, our staff contribute to our critical mission of ending suicide among lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer & questioning young people by embodying our values:

Heart - We care deeply and commit to do what matters

Integrity - We build trust through our words and actions

Community - Together, we are an unstoppable force for good

Belonging - We serve as allies to the mission, and to each other

Progress - We continually move onward and upward

Applicants and staff must be authorized to work for any employer in the United States. The Trevor Project will not sponsor an employment visa for this position.

Role: Copywriter
Location: This role will be remote in the continental United States, Alaska, or Hawaii
Reports to: Sr. Director of Marketing & Content
Eligibility: Authorization to work for any employer in the United States is required. The Trevor Project will not sponsor a work visa for this role.
Classification: Exempt Full Time
Union Role? Yes
Starting Salary Range: $68,640/yr

Summary:
The Trevor Project is looking to hire a copyediting and copywriting specialist to unify our brand voice and create owned content for our audience of more than 2 million people. Reporting to the Sr. Director of Content, this person will be responsible for bringing our brand to life through creative copy across all platforms and providing copyediting support for teams making content requests for external facing communications. They will work within the Marketing & Content department and coordinate with teams across the Advancement vertical and larger organization to understand and support the current and future strategic direction of the organization. We’re looking for someone who is creative and dynamic, with experience copywriting and copyediting for SEO, email marketing, brand marketing, and social marketing. Your work will help connect us authentically to LGBTQ+ youth and the adults who support the efforts of our organization, helping you be a part of our mission in saving LGBTQ+ young lives.

Roles and Responsibilities:

Manage the drafting of copy across various platforms based on owned marketing activations, campaigns, and cross-team project support requests

Check the work of internal and external communications for any grammatical errors or typos, ensuring we are always putting out only the highest quality work and match the overall brand tone and voice

Work with internal subject matter experts to create educational resources, such as handbooks and guides, as well as social-first promotional copy for each resource

Work with our video editor to draft scripts for organic and paid social activations and campaigns

Write blogs and other online resources optimized for SEO, AEO, and GEO

Find creative ways to develop our brand voice, work with our data to identify what is/isn’t working well, and iterate on what can be improved upon

Demonstrate fair, ethical, and equitable business practices

Handle sensitive and confidential matters/ data with the utmost discretion and integrity

Learn eagerly, share knowledge appropriately, and improve continuously

Demonstrate successful planning and problem-solving skills, including multitasking and working well within tight timelines

Work, communicate, and collaborate effectively with others

Demonstrate attention to detail and accuracy in all work

Demonstrate a commitment to fostering and maintaining an environment of belonging

Other relevant duties and responsibilities as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:

Minimum 2 years experience copywriting and copyediting in an agency or internal team environment for brand marketing

Proficiency in:

LGBTQ+ topics and discourse in media

Google Suite (GMail, Drive, Docs, Slides, Sheets)

Content development, including self-directed research and interviews, to support the creation of videos, guides, blogs, op-eds, and other brand related content

Giving and receiving constructive feedback in a collaborative work environment

Project management (Trevor uses Asana)

Proficiency in spoken and written English

Why Trevor?

Join a mission-driven organization where your work makes a real impact on the lives of LGBTQ+ young people—every single day.

Outstanding benefits, including:

- Comprehensive health coverage, including plans that support various gender affirmation care needs

- Mental health resources, with access to virtual care and a variety of in and out of network options for support

- 403(b) retirement plan with a 3% employer match, vesting over three years

- Generous paid time off and company holidays to rest and recharge

job_description:
About the Role

We're looking for a B2B Copywriter to join our team and contribute to one of our most significant enterprise engagements: Amazon Web Services. This role sits inside the AWS content program, working closely with the Lead Content Strategist and Senior B2B Copywriter to bring strategy and briefs to life through clear, polished copy.

You'll be hands-on across a range of formats, learning to write for technical and executive B2B audiences while building craft under the guidance of more senior creatives on the account.

What You'll Do
• Write B2B content based on established briefs, creative strategy, and messaging frameworks.
• Translate technical input and source material into clear, accurate copy.
• Follow AWS's brand voice, style guide, and messaging architecture consistently across deliverables.
• Incorporate feedback from the Senior Copywriter and Lead Content Strategist, revising efficiently and applying notes to future work.
• Support content production across multiple formats and a steady volume of deliverables, meeting deadlines without sacrificing quality.
• Conduct basic research to ensure accuracy on technical and industry topics (cloud infrastructure, AI/ML, developer tools).

• Strong attention to detail and reliability with deadlines.

job_description:
Tenstorrent is leading the industry on cutting-edge AI technology, revolutionizing performance expectations, ease of use, and cost efficiency. With AI redefining the computing paradigm, solutions must evolve to unify innovations in software models, compilers, platforms, networking, and semiconductors. Our diverse team of technologists have developed a high performance RISC-V CPU from scratch, and share a passion for AI and a deep desire to build the best AI platform possible.

We value collaboration, curiosity, and a commitment to solving hard problems. We are growing our team and looking for contributors of all seniorities.

At Tenstorrent, we build computers for AI, and the developers shaping its future.

Our high-performance RISC-V CPUs, modular chiplets, and scalable compute systems give developers full control at every layer of the stack, at any scale from a single-node experimentation to data center-scale deployment.

We believe in an open future. Our architecture and software are designed to be edited, forked, and owned. Our team of engineers, dreamers, and first-principle thinkers is redefining how hardware and software converge to accelerate innovation.

Tenstorrent's comms team is growing, and the timing isn't an accident. We have multiple products coming to market, new solutions in the pipeline, and a story that the industry is paying attention to. We're looking for a copywriter who can help us tell it – in our own way. You’ll get your hands dirty working across a myriad of content. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is comfortable stepping in, rolling up their sleeves, and writing to shape the future.

• This role is remote, based out of the United States.
• We welcome candidates at various experience levels for this role. During the interview process, candidates will be assessed for the appropriate level, and offers will align with that level, which may differ from the one in this posting.
• *Who You Are
• You follow AI and hardware closely enough to have opinions.
• You write like you talk: clear, direct, no fluff.
• You have a sixth sense for when a sentence will make an engineer roll their eyes.
• You simplify hard things without dumbing them down.
• You know when to ask questions or where to search for answers.
• *What We Need
• 3+ years of copywriting experience, ideally in tech or a technical-adjacent industry.
• Demonstrated ability to write for both technical and business audiences.
• A portfolio that shows range, from social posts to long form content and everything in between.
• Familiarity with the AI/ML landscape (hardware knowledge is a plus, not a dealbreaker).
• Strong instincts for editing your own work (less is usually more).
• *What You Will Learn**
• The inner workings of a state of the art AI company and why the compute architecture decisions being made right now will define the next decade.
• Translating genuinely novel technology into stories that land with developers, enterprises, and everyone in between.
• Using comms as a tool to support a growing product focused AI company.
• Creating and maintaining a brand one sentence at a time.
• A new level of transparency that comes with a company building an Open Future.
• Compensation for all engineers at Tenstorrent ranges from $100k - $500k including base and variable compensation targets. Experience, skills, education, background and location all impact the actual offer made.
• Tenstorrent offers a highly competitive compensation package and benefits, and we are an equal opportunity employer.
• This offer of employment is contingent upon the applicant being eligible to access U.S. export-controlled technology. Due to U.S. export laws, including those codified in the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), the Company is required to ensure compliance with these laws when transferring technology to nationals of certain countries (such as EAR Country Groups D:1, E1, and E2). These requirements apply to persons located in the U.S. and all countries outside the U.S. As the position offered will have direct and/or indirect access to information, systems, or technologies subject to these laws, the offer may be contingent upon your citizenship/permanent residency status or ability to obtain prior license approval from the U.S. Commerce Department or applicable federal agency. If employment is not possible due to U.S. export laws, any offer of employment will be rescinded.*

job_description:
Multiply is an agency brands hire to fight boring.

Our clients? Leading brands in the beverage and food CPG sector. We're a team of elite creatives who deliver strategy grounded in business objectives, insights rooted in consumer and market research, and world-class creative across earned, social, digital, experiential,TV/video, out of home and in-store.

We’re nice people seeking nicer people—and these are the values we live by:

• Happy - A place where people genuinely want to come to work
• Daring - We value hard work and bold creativity that moves the needle
• People - We take care of each other and invest in growth

DESCRIPTION

Multiply is seeking a Junior Copywriter to join our beverage and consumer brands PR team. You'll craft compelling copy across earned and owned channels—from press releases and social content to campaign messaging and brand storytelling. Working alongside our senior copywriters and creative teams, you'll develop your voice while learning how to translate brand strategy and consumer insights into words that cut through the noise.

This is an ideal role for a sharp, ambitious writer who's eager to grow in a fast-paced agency environment and contribute to work that helps brands earn attention the right way.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

• Develop copy for a variety of client deliverables including press releases, social media content, email campaigns, brand messaging, and earned media assets. Ensure all copy aligns with client brand tone, voice guidelines, and strategic positioning while maintaining grammatical excellence and strong storytelling.

• Collaborate closely with Art Directors, designers, and account teams to ensure copy complements visual concepts and strengthens overall creative execution. Work within design constraints and platform specifications, adapting messaging for different channels and formats (Instagram captions, TikTok scripts, headlines, body copy, etc.)

• Participate actively in brainstorms and creative ideation sessions, contributing original ideas and helping shape campaign concepts. Present copy concepts to team members and, with guidance, to clients, articulating the strategic thinking behind messaging choices.

• Support account teams with daily content needs, including social media copy, caption writing, and reactive content opportunities. Manage multiple projects simultaneously, meeting tight deadlines without compromising quality or brand consistency.
• Research client brands, competitive landscapes, and consumer trends to inform messaging and storytelling. Stay current with beverage and CPG industry news, cultural moments, and social media trends that present opportunities for timely, relevant content.

• Take direction efficiently, incorporating feedback and revisions while maintaining strategic intent. Learn and internalize client brand guidelines, ensuring consistency across all written deliverables.

• Proofread all copy developed by team members and assist Sr. Copywriter in maintaining quality standards across accounts. Flag potential brand or tone inconsistencies and suggest improvements.

• Participate in new business pitches, contributing copy samples and helping showcase the agency's writing capabilities to prospective clients.

QUALIFICATIONS

• You're a thinker, an idea person, creative to the bone.
• Bachelor's degree and/or master’s degree in Communications, Advertising, Marketing, English, Journalism, or related field (or equivalent professional writing experience)
• 1-2 years of copywriting, marketing, or communications experience in an agency, brand, or media environment
• Your portfolio shows you’ve written for brands with creative zingers across multiple formats (social media, long-form, headlines, product copy, etc.) and ability to write in different brand voices
• Excellent command of grammar, spelling, and AP Style; meticulous attention to detail with ability to catch errors in your own and others' work
• Understanding of social media platforms and how copy performs differently across Instagram, TikTok, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, and other channels
• Familiarity with beverage, food, CPG, or consumer lifestyle brands; genuine interest in these categories
• Ability to take creative direction well, accept feedback constructively, and iterate quickly without losing confidence
• Strong collaboration skills and ability to communicate ideas clearly with designers, account managers, and creative leadership
• Quick learner who can absorb brand guidelines and client specifications rapidly
• Enthusiasm for pop culture, trends, and cultural moments that could inform brand storytelling
• Ability to manage multiple projects and shifting priorities in a fast-paced environment while maintaining quality

COMPANY BENEFITS

• 100% Remote, within NYC metro area preferred
• 100% paid Health insurance
• Dental, Vision, Life, Disability insurance options with access to mental health advisors
• FSA/HSA options
• 401(k) program
• Weekly Wellness Hours
• Unlimited Vacation Days
• 11 Company Holidays
• Closed Between Dec. 23 and Jan. 2
• Quarterly Retreats
• Best in class technology and systems
