job_title: Remote Junior Copywriter - Digital Marketing Focus
job_description:
Job Summary

The Elite Job is seeking a creative and motivated Remote Junior Copywriter with a strong focus on digital marketing to join our dynamic content team. This entry-level role offers the opportunity to craft compelling, clear, and engaging copy across multiple digital platforms, including social media, email campaigns, websites, and paid advertisements. You will collaborate closely with marketing specialists, designers, and SEO teams to create content that drives engagement, builds brand awareness, and supports lead generation. This role is perfect for someone looking to develop their writing skills within a supportive, fast-paced digital marketing environment.
Key Responsibilities
• *

Write clear, persuasive, and original copy tailored for various digital marketing channels such as social media posts, email newsletters, blog articles, PPC ads, and landing pages.
• *

Collaborate with marketing and design teams to brainstorm and develop creative concepts that align with campaign goals and brand voice.
• *

Assist in editing and proofreading content to ensure grammatical accuracy, clarity, and consistency with brand guidelines.
• *

Conduct research on industry trends, competitors, and target audiences to inform and enhance content strategies.
• *

Adapt copy to optimize for SEO best practices and improve organic search performance.
• *

Manage multiple projects and deadlines effectively, maintaining high standards for quality and timely delivery.
• *

Stay up to date with digital marketing trends and tools to continuously improve content effectiveness.
• Required Skills and Qualifications
• *

Bachelors degree in English, Communications, Marketing, Journalism, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
• *

Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills with a strong command of the English language.
• *

Basic understanding of digital marketing principles including SEO, social media, email marketing, and PPC.
• *

Ability to write in different tones and styles depending on the target audience and platform.
• *

Familiarity with content management systems (CMS) such as WordPress is a plus.
• *

Strong attention to detail and the ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
• *

Good organizational skills and ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously.
• *

Proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets) and basic knowledge of marketing tools is advantageous.
• Experience
• *

0-2 years of professional writing experience, preferably in digital marketing or related fields.
• *

Internship or freelance writing experience in content creation is highly valued.
• Working Hours
• *

Full-time, Monday to Friday.
• *

Flexible working hours with the requirement to overlap some hours with the core team for collaboration.
• *

Remote work setup, allowing work from anywhere with a reliable internet connection.
• Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
• *

Strong storytelling and creative thinking abilities.
• *

Ability to absorb feedback and iterate quickly on copy drafts.
• *

Basic understanding of analytics and how to measure content performance is a plus.
• *

Proactive attitude with a passion for learning and growth in the digital marketing space.
• Benefits
• *

Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
• *

Work-from-home flexibility with a supportive remote team culture.
• *

Access to ongoing training and professional development programs.
• *

Opportunity to work with industry leaders and grow your career in digital marketing.
• *

Paid time off and holidays.
• *

Inclusive and diverse workplace environment.
• Why Join The Elite Job?

At The Elite Job, we believe in nurturing talent and providing opportunities for growth. As part of our team, you will be empowered to develop your skills, collaborate on exciting projects, and contribute meaningfully to impactful marketing campaigns. We foster a culture of innovation, creativity, and mutual respect, ensuring our employees feel valued and motivated. If you are eager to jumpstart your copywriting career in digital marketing and thrive in a flexible remote setting, this is the perfect role for you.
How to Apply

Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume, a cover letter, and a portfolio or samples of relevant writing work to us with the subject line Remote Junior Copywriter Application. Please ensure your application clearly demonstrates your writing skills and passion for digital marketing.

job_description:
Company Overview:

When it comes to IT solution providers, there are a lot of choices. But when it comes to providers with innovative and differentiating end-to-end service offerings, there’s really only one: Zones – First Choice for IT.TM

Zones is a Global Solution Provider of end-to-end IT solutions with an unmatched supply chain. Positioned to be the IT partner you need, Zones, a Minority Business Enterprise (MBE) in business for over 35 years, specializes in Digital Workplace, Cloud & Data Center, Networking, Security, and Managed/Professional/Staffing services. Operating in more than 120 countries, leveraging a robust portfolio, and utilizing the highest certification levels from key partners, including Microsoft, Apple, Cisco, Lenovo, Adobe, and more, Zones has mastered the science of building digital infrastructures that change the way business does business ensuring whatever they need, they can Consider IT Done. Follow Zones, LLC on Twitter @Zones, and LinkedIn and Facebook.

Position Overview:

We are seeking a skilled and experienced Copywriter I to join our marketing team. As a Copywriter, you will be responsible for crafting compelling and engaging copy for various marketing channels and campaigns. You will work to ensure all content is on-brand, consistent in style, quality-driven, and aligns with our company's messaging, tone and voice, and company goals. The ideal candidate should have a proven track record of creating impactful copy, strong communication skills, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.

The position is remote based in the United States, with a preferred location in Seattle or Chicago.

What you will do as a Copywriter I:

The requirements below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. This job description is not intended to be a comprehensive list of the duties and responsibilities of the position, and the duties and responsibilities may change without notice.

• Conceptualize and develop creative and persuasive copy for marketing campaigns, advertisements, websites, emails, social media, corporate collateral, and other channels.
• Collaborate closely with designers, Integrated Marketing, Website team, and other teams to understand project requirements, target audience, and objectives.
• Edit and proofread copy to ensure clarity, grammar, consistency, and brand voice and tone adherence.
• Understand and follow AP Style Guides for copy consistency and adherence.
• Stay updated on industry trends and best practices in copywriting and digital marketing. Knowledge of the IT technology industry is a bonus.
• Conduct research and interviews to understand customer needs and market trends.
• Follow an established content calendar to ensure timely delivery of materials.
• Follow an established copy standards playbook to adhere to content consistency.

What you will bring to the team:

• 1+ years of proven experience as a Copywriter in B2B technology companies, IT/AV distribution or reseller preferred.
• Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills.
• Ability to adapt writing style to various tones, voices, and platforms.
• Excellent organizational and time-management skills.
• Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, or related field (preferred).
• Proficiency in SEO principles and content optimization.
• Excellent project management and organizational skills with the ability to prioritize and multitask in a fast-paced environment.
• Proven ability to work closely and successfully with a sales organization.
• Ability to work very independently and collaborate with cross-functional teams.
• Rigorous attention to detail and focus on quality of deliverables.
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships across sales, marketing and external vendors and associates.
Qualified candidates can expect a hourly rate of $24.03.

Zones offers a comprehensive Benefits package

While we’re committed to providing top-tier solutions, we’re just as committed to supporting our own team. Our team members enjoy a variety of comprehensive benefits, including medical, dental and vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, voluntary accident, hospitalization and critical illness insurance options, a 401(k) plan with matching provision, and many more. Generally, Zones currently offers paid time off and personal sick leave in compliance to individual state requirements.

At Zones, work is more than a job – it is an exciting career on a global team that is client centric, has a passion for technology, embraces change and lifelong learning in a collaborative culture. And as a Minority Business Enterprise, a Corporate Plus member of the Northwest Minority Supplier Development Council, and an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer, our community is just as diverse. job_description: Copywriting Jobs No Experience – Become a Remote Copywriter and Earn $25-$35/hr Job Overview Are you interested in becoming a copywriter but don’t have prior experience? job_title: Entertainment Media Content Copy Writer (Remote in US)
job_description:
This opportunity is only available to candidates currently residing in the following U.S. states: Alabama, Colorado, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Missouri, Montana, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Mexico, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, West Virginia, and Wisconsin.

Overview

Help shape how AI talks about TV and film.

We’re looking for writers with genuine entertainment fluency — people who actually watch anime, K-drama, dark comedy, atmospheric horror, and family cinema, and can write about it in a polished, on-brand voice.

You’ll work from home on a flexible schedule.

What You Will Do

• Write short-form content about TV shows, films, and entertainment titles in a defined brand voice
• Rewrite AI-generated entertainment copy so it sounds natural, accurate, and tonally right for the genre
• Research plot details, cast, episodes, and cultural context to keep every line factually correct
• Match tone to the title — a horror logline shouldn’t read like a rom-com summary
• Flag AI responses that miss genre conventions, get facts wrong, or feel off in voice

Project details

• Start date: Immediate
• Duration: Ongoing
• Hours: :20 hours per week, flexible schedule
• Job type: Freelance contract
• Location: Remote, US-based
• Rate: $40/h

Who We Are Looking For

• A real writer — short-form copy, editorial, brand, fiction, screenwriting, or published reviews. Not “writing-adjacent” work. A portfolio link, clippings, Substack, or published samples should accompany your application.
• Genuine entertainment fluency — If you can tell us, off the top of your head, the difference between isekai and shōnen, name a comedian whose set you’d recommend to a friend, or explain why a K-drama like Destined With You works, you’re the right person.
• Native or near-native conversational US English, with strong control of tone, grammar, and register. Comfortable writing inside a brand voice — you’ve written as a publication, brand, or persona before, not just in your own voice.
• A background in English, Creative Writing, Journalism, Film/Media Studies, Theatre, or Communications is a plus, not a requirement.
• Ready for structured short-form work with character limits, style guides, and a high accuracy bar.
evaluation_reason: This role is a poor fit primarily due to the strict 'Remote in US' location requirement, which conflicts with your British time zone and residency. job_description:
Who We Are

Drop is a data-driven product design company built by and for enthusiasts. Founded in San Francisco in 2012, Drop is home to 7 million members across the globe who actively inform our product design engine. Drop combines deep industry expertise, community insights, and rich data signals to help create high-quality Mechanical Keyboard and Audiophile products that exceed the expectations of our communities. Join the community at Drop.com.

About the Role

Drop is looking for a Copywriter to provide ongoing copywriting work for various Drop marketing projects including product, social and email copy. We’re a fast-growing, fast-paced e-commerce startup, and our members are our first priority. The Copywriter will work closely with the Creative team and report directly to our Art Director.

This is a part-time contractor position for 10-20 hours per week with the potential to become a more permanent role.

Drop is a distributed workplace which means none of us work out of an office. We’re looking to expand beyond our San Francisco-based team to include great remote talent located anywhere in the US or Canada.

Key Responsibilities

• Provide compelling copy for projects including: product pages, email, growth marketing and other materials.

• Communicate complicated technical topics in clear, concise & compelling ways to a both a non-technical audience & our highly technical enthusiast base

• Providing feedback and editing other team members’ work and helping to update the company’s style guide.

• Work collaboratively within a cross-functional team environment with art director, marketing, engineers, strategists, and product managers

• Work autonomously, with little hand-holding

• Create original, breakthrough work within established brand guidelines

Qualifications

• Bachelor's degree in journalism, creative writing, or another field that requires consistent writing

• 5+ years of professional experience writing copy in an in-house Creative department or comparable creative agency

• Experience writing copy in the consumer electronics space is a plus.

To apply, send us your resume and 2-3 relevant writing samples.

Follow us

Find and follow your passions on drop.com and the Drop app for iPhone and Android smartphones. You can also connect with @drop on Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter.

Funded by KPCB, First Round Capital, Mayfield 50, and August Capital.

Drop is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
evaluation_reason: This remote role is limited to US/Canada, which directly contradicts the need for a British Time Zone alignment. Additionally, the job's focus on consumer electronics (mechanical keyboards, audiophile products) is a complete mismatch for your desired content niches like Wellness and Personal Development.

job_description:
Western Growers Health─ a part of Western Growers Family of Companies-provides employer-sponsored health benefit plans to meet the needs of those working for the agriculture industry. The unmatched benefit options provided by Western Growers Health stem from the core mission of Western Growers Association (est. 1926) to support the business interests of employers in the agriculture industry.

Our mission at Western Growers Health is to deliver value to employers by offering robust health plans that meet the needs of a diverse workforce. By working at Western Growers Health, you will join a dedicated team of employees who care about offering quality health benefits and excellent customer service to plan participants. If you want to start making a difference working in the health care industry, then apply to Western Growers Health today!  
Compensation: $64,967.09 - $84,458.07 with a rich benefits package that includes profit-sharing.

JOB DESCRIPTION SUMMARY
The Healthcare Copywriter reports to the Director, Member Engagement & Communications and is responsible for developing and managing corporate communications and member-facing content that educate and inform individuals about their health benefits. A key focus of this position is creating clear, culturally relevant, and accessible content that simplifies complex insurance concepts for individuals who may be new to health benefits. The ideal candidate is an expert communicator, a skilled copywriter, and fluent in both English and Spanish, with experience crafting messaging that resonates with different audiences.

QUALIFICATIONS
• Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) and four (4) to six (6) years of work experience in copywriting, content marketing, or corporate communications for healthcare or employee benefits, preferred
• Fluent in Spanish and English (spoken and written) is required; experience in bilingual copywriting preferred.
• Strong copywriting skills with experience crafting compelling marketing copy.
• Professional portfolio showcasing compelling copywriting and marketing work capabilities
• Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills with strong attention to detail.
• Excellent oral communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
• Proven ability to simplify complex topics into engaging, easy-to-understand content.
• Strong understanding of health literacy, best practices, and experience writing for diverse
populations. Experience with content strategy, messaging development, and brand storytelling.
• Strong project management skills with the ability to multitask and meet deadlines. Proficiency in Microsoft Office and content management systems (CMS); familiarity with marketing automation, preferred.
• Knowledge of compliance and regulatory requirements related to healthcare communications, preferred.
• Knowledge of Search Engine Optimization (SEO) best practices, email marketing, and digital content best practices is a plus.
• Internet access provided by a cable or fiber provider with 40 MB download and 10 MB upload speeds.
• Home router with wired Ethernet (wireless connections and hotspots are not permitted).
• A designated room for your office or steps taken to protect company information (e.g., facing computer towards wall, etc.)
• A functioning smoke detector, fire extinguisher, and first aid kit on site.

DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Content Development & Copywriting
• Write, edit, and proofread high-quality content for corporate communications and member education materials in both English and Spanish.
• Develop member-facing materials such as welcome guides, FAQs, newsletters, website content, email campaigns, social media posts, and printed brochures to explain health benefits.
• Create engaging, culturally appropriate messaging that considers literacy levels, language nuances, and member needs.
• Translate complex insurance concepts into simple, easy-to-understand language that empowers members to utilize their benefits.
• Ensure all communications adhere to brand voice, tone, and style guidelines while maintaining accuracy and clarity.
• Collaborate with designers, developers, and marketing colleagues to create compelling visual and written content.
Bilingual & Cultural Communication
• Develop Spanish-language content that is not just a direct translation but is culturally relevant and audience-appropriate.
• Work closely with member-facing teams to understand key concerns and barriers for Spanish[1]speaking populations.
• Ensure that health literacy best practices are applied to all communications, making insurance concepts more accessible and actionable.
• Support education campaigns that increase member awareness and utilization of health benefits.
• Assist with the development of strategic marketing campaigns that support member retention and engagement efforts.
• Support community engagement initiatives with language that builds partnerships and engages diverse audiences.
• Analyze member feedback and engagement metrics to continuously improve content effectiveness.
Corporate & Internal Communications
• Support corporate messaging and executive communications, including internal announcements, leadership updates, press releases, and presentations.
• Partner with Sales, Account Management, Customer Service, and other departments to develop effective internal and external communications.
• Manage and refine crisis communication strategies to ensure timely and transparent messaging.
• Work with legal and compliance teams to review materials for accuracy and regulatory compliance.
Other
• Utilize all capabilities to satisfy one mission — to enhance the competitiveness and profitability of our members. Do everything possible to help members succeed by being curious and striving to understand what others are trying to achieve, planning, and executing work helpfully and collaboratively. Be willing to adjust efforts to ensure that work and attitude are helpful to others, be self-accountable, create a positive impact, and be diligent in delivering results.
• Maintain internet speed of 40 MB download and 10 MB upload and router with wired Ethernet.
• Maintain a HIPAA-compliant workstation and utilize appropriate security techniques to ensure HIPAA required protection of all confidential/protected client data.
• Internet access provided by a cable or fiber provider with 40 MB download and 10 MB upload speeds.
• Home router with wired Ethernet (wireless connections and hotspots are not permitted).
• A designated room for your office or steps taken to protect company information (e.g., facing computer towards wall, etc.)
• Maintain and service safety equipment (e.g., smoke detector, fire extinguisher, first aid kit).
• All other duties as assigned.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS/WORK ENVIRONMENT
The physical demands and work environment described here represent those that an employee must meet to successfully perform this job’s essential functions. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to communicate with others. The employee frequently is required to move around the office. The employee is often required to use tools, objects, and controls. This noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

job_description:
iPullRank is an ambitious ten-year-old digital marketing agency based in New York City, founded by industry thought leader Michael King. Our mission is to drive results for our clients by delivering marketing work that we can be PROUD of. Oh, and our value set is PROUD: P – Proactive; R – Reliable; O – Outstanding; U – Useful; D – Dedicated. We keep that front and center on a day-to-day basis.

If you’re curious about what types of clients we work with, they range from Fortune 500 brands to mid-sized enterprises and exciting small businesses. Our team executes and consults on campaigns in various industries, but many of our clients are media, finance, or fashion companies. The iPullRank team is adept at providing a range of digital marketing services, but most of our work is in some combination of Content Strategy, Search Engine Optimization (SEO), Analytics and Conversion Rate Optimization. Our employees are duly encouraged to specialize deeply in a specific category they are passionate about and grow into well-rounded marketers through collaboration, training, and real-world experience.

As an employer, we cultivate an environment where employees feel cared for. Despite our size, we offer competitive salaries and benefits. We want our employees to grow with us. We don’t want you just to fill a role, we want you to take ownership and make an impact.

We’re not a big agency, and our clients appreciate that because our size allows us to make things happen. We’re a nimble group that believes the best idea can come from anyone in the company—and we’re always after the best idea. We’re looking for creative and smart people to join our team and help us find that best idea. If that’s you, we’d love to hear from you.

Position Description

iPullRank is looking for a content writer to join our team and help with various internal marketing projects and client work. This role will prepare long- and short-form copy for a variety of brands and must be willing to understand how to master different voices and tones. This role collaborates with our content strategy team to execute on strategic plans and our research and analytics teams on optimizing copy through conversion testing.

The ideal applicant has the ability to seamlessly transition between persuasive advertising copy and editorial copy. This role is US-based only. Salary range: $65-75K

What You’ll Do

• Prepare metadata for content experiences

• Write a variety of content types, from blog posts and long-form resource articles to landing pages and ebooks

• Prepare copy for client deliverables

• Prepare copy for client content experiences

• Prepare copy for social media status updates

• Prepare copy for wireframes and mockups

• Brainstorm content ideas with content strategy team

• Edit copy from clients and other content creation teams

• Enter updated copy into Content Management Systems

• Work directly with analysts and content strategists to execute on content campaigns

• Work with the Research & Analytics team to design and execute Conversion tests

Requirements

• 2-5 years of experience in a similar copywriting position

• Proven experience writing short-form and long-form content for blogs and resource centers

• Previous SEO experience, including writing articles with the intent of driving search traffic

• Experience writing copy for various verticals and multiple brand voices and tones

• Handles stressful situations and deadline pressures well

• Must possess a high degree of accuracy and pay close attention to detail (e.g. no typos)

• Strong analytical mindset and skills, excellent problem-solving skills

• Possess strong written, analytical and verbal skills

• Excellent written and oral communication skills

• High level of initiative and work well in a team environment

• Proficient in Microsoft Office applications such as Word, Excel and PowerPoint

• Plans and carries out responsibilities with minimal direction

Nice to have

• Experience entering copy into Content Management Systems a plus

• Prior Experience with Optimizely, Unbounce or similar A/B Testing platforms a plus

• Experience with ClickUp a plus

Benefits

• Competitive salaries commensurate with experience

• 100% Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance

• 3 weeks of vacation

• Paid sick days and holidays
evaluation_reason: While Natalie's core copywriting and content strategy skills are an excellent match for the role, the critical 'Remote US' constraint and typical client industries (media, finance, fashion) are a significant mismatch for her 'British Time Zone' requirement and preferred niche (wellness, personal development).

job_title: Lead Copywriter job at Darn Tough Vermont in Waterbury, VT
job_description:
Lead Copywriter - Waterbury, VT

Job Category: MARKETING

Requisition Number: LEADC001469

Posting Details

Full-Time

Locations

Waterbury
Darn Tough Vermont
150 Pilgrim Park Rd
Waterbury, VT 05676, USA

Job Details

Description

The Company:

Founded in 1978, Cabot Hosiery Mills is an American manufacturer of premium socks with uncompromised comfort, durability, and fit. With a family of brands including Darn Tough Vermont, Wide Open, and Cabot & Sons, our mission is to make the world’s best socks, in Vermont, USA, and back them with our Unconditional Lifetime Guarantee. We are family owned, three generations strong, and have yet to produce our best sock.

Job Summary:

As we continue to scale one of the fastest growing apparel brands, we are seeking a strategic, hands-on Lead Copywriter to set messaging direction and define how the brand communicates and tells its story across campaigns and consumer touchpoints. This role owns messaging strategy, concept development, and copy standards; ensuring brand voice and tone are clear, cohesive, and impactful across all initiatives. The role provides daily guidance, feedback and instruction to lower level copywriters to ensure brand voice is consistent and effective.

Working in close partnership with the Director of Content + Creative, Manager of Corporate Communications, and creative, marketing, and cross-functional teams, the Lead Copywriter shapes and evolves the brand’s voice, narrative, and messaging frameworks to support brand and business objectives.

The Lead Copywriter leads copy from concept through execution, setting the standard for quality, consistency, and effectiveness. This role mentor’s copywriters, manages workflows, and elevates the overall strength, cohesion, and strategic impact of brand messaging.

the starting salary for this role is $90,000-$105,000 based on experience

Key Responsibilities:

Messaging Strategy & Creative Development

Lead messaging concept development and strategy for brand and product campaigns across all channels.
Establish, shape, evolve, and uphold brand voice, tone, messaging standards, and copy systems to ensure consistency and clarity.
Translate business and marketing objectives into compelling messaging frameworks and creative concepts.
Lead copy development from initial concept through execution, ensuring alignment with approved creative direction.
Clearly articulate messaging rationale, decisions, and strategic intent to internal teams and stakeholders.
Contribute to naming, storytelling, product messaging, campaign narratives, and brand defining initiatives.
Ensure messaging maintains cohesion and impact across all channels.

Team Mentorship

Provide direction, mentorship, and feedback to copywriters, fostering growth in craft, creative thinking, and strategic messaging.
Delegate work effectively across internal and freelance resources, ensuring quality, consistency, and efficiency.
Foster a collaborative, solutions-oriented environment that encourages creative excellence and accountability.
Proactively manage feedback, resolve challenges, and maintain smooth messaging workflows across projects.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement

Partner closely with the Director of Content + Creative, Design Lead, Manager of Corporate Communications, Marketing, and Project Management to deliver cohesive, on-brand copy.
Collaborate with designers, content creators, and external partners to ensure alignment between messaging and visual execution.
Present messaging concepts clearly and confidently, incorporating feedback while maintaining messaging integrity.
Support leadership, including Founder and strategic initiatives, with thoughtful, brand aligned messaging.
Partner with Project Management to ensure copy deliverables are completed on time, on brief, and on brand.

Brand Stewardship & Quality

Own messaging decisions and executional standards, ensuring all copy aligns with established brand voice and messaging frameworks.
Maintain consistency and cohesion across creative outputs, identifying and resolving messaging inconsistencies.
Continuously refine and elevate messaging standards, processes, and copy output quality.
Contribute to the evolution of brand narrative and messaging systems as the brand grows and expands.

7–10+ years of professional copywriting experience, with a strong portfolio of brand and campaign work.
A bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or equivalent experience

Highly organized, collaborative, proactive, and solutions oriented.

Messaging across all channels is clear, cohesive, compelling, and consistently aligned with brand voice and standards.

Messaging direction helps define and strengthen the brand narrative as the company grows.

Working Conditions:

Office Environment – some exposure to manufacturing environment
Hybrid schedule – ability to be on site at least 3 days a week
Flexible nature to manage competing and changing priorities
Occasional travel on as needed basis

We’re an honest group of people and work hard, if you like that, we encourage you to apply.

job_title: Copywriter job at Charlie Health in New York, NY
job_description:
Title: Copywriter

Location: New York ,United States

Job Description:

Why Charlie Health?

Millions of people across the country are navigating mental health conditions, substance use disorders, and eating disorders, but too often, they’re met with barriers to care. From limited local options and long wait times to treatment that lacks personalization, behavioral healthcare can leave people feeling unseen and unsupported.

Charlie Health exists to change that. Our mission is to connect the world to life-saving behavioral health treatment. We deliver personalized, virtual care rooted in connection—between clients and clinicians, care teams, loved ones, and the communities that support them. By focusing on people with complex needs, we’re expanding access to meaningful care and driving better outcomes from the comfort of home.

As a rapidly growing organization, we're reaching more communities every day and building a team that’s redefining what behavioral health treatment can look like. If you're ready to use your skills to drive lasting change and help more people access the care they deserve, we’d love to meet you.

About the Role

Charlie Health is seeking a Product Marketing Copywriter to support the development of high-impact, clarity-driven content across our marketing ecosystem. This role will partner closely with Product Marketing, Design and Growth teams to translate complex behavioral health programs into clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate copy.

You’ll be responsible for writing and refining copy across website pages, whitepapers, case studies, printable assets, and sales collateral, ensuring our value is communicated with empathy, precision, and consistency. This is a highly collaborative role ideal for a writer who enjoys working at the intersection of storytelling and healthcare.

The ideal candidate is a strong writer and editor who can adapt tone across audiences (clients, caregivers, providers, partners), synthesize feedback from multiple stakeholders, and bring structure and polish to both short- and long-form content. Experience in healthcare, behavioral health, or regulated industries is a plus, but strong product marketing instincts and editorial judgment are essential.

We’re a team of passionate, forward-thinking professionals eager to take on the challenge of the mental health crisis and play a formative role in providing life-saving solutions. If you’re inspired by our mission and energized by the opportunity to increase access to mental healthcare and impact millions of lives in a profound way, apply today.

Responsibilities

Write and edit high-quality copy for website pages, including program overviews, condition pages, and conversion-oriented content
Develop long-form assets such as whitepapers, outcomes reports, and case studies that balance narrative storytelling with data and proof points
Support creation of printable and field assets (rack cards, trifolds, one-pagers, two-pagers) in partnership with PMM and Design
Translate Product Marketing strategy, positioning, and packaging frameworks into clear, compelling copy across formats
Adapt messaging for multiple audiences, including clients, caregivers, providers, referral partners, and internal teams
Collaborate cross-functionally with PMMs, designers, clinicians, and leadership to incorporate feedback and ensure accuracy
Edit and refine existing content to improve clarity, flow, tone, and consistency with brand guidelines
Maintain a strong understanding of Charlie Health’s programs, audiences, and differentiators to ensure messaging accuracy and alignment
Support PMM workflows by helping scale output across multiple initiatives and launches simultaneously

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, English, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
2+ years of experience in copywriting, content marketing, or product marketing, preferably in healthcare, mental health, or another regulated industry
Demonstrated experience writing both short-form and long-form content, including websites, whitepapers, case studies, or sales collateral
Strong ability to adapt tone and messaging across different audiences and use cases
Excellent editorial judgment with a proven ability to simplify complex topics without losing nuance
Comfort working cross-functionally and incorporating feedback from multiple stakeholders
Please note that members of this team who live within 45 minutes of a Charlie Health office are expected to adhere to a hybrid work schedule

Benefits

Charlie Health is pleased to offer comprehensive benefits to all full-time, exempt employees. Read more about our benefits here.

The total target base compensation for this role will be between $75,000 and $100,000 per year at the commencement of employment. In addition to base compensation, this role also offers a performance bonus. The expected total cash compensation range, including potential bonus, will be between $82,000 and $116,000 per year. Please note, pay will be determined on an individualized basis and will be impacted by location, experience, expertise, internal pay equity, and other relevant business considerations. Further, cash compensation is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may include stock options and other Charlie Health-sponsored benefits. #LI-HYBRID

Our Values

Connection: Care deeply & inspire hope.
Congruence: Stay curious & heed the evidence.
Commitment: Act with urgency & don’t give up.

Please do not call our public clinical admissions line in regard to this or any other job posting.

Please be cautious of potential recruitment fraud. If you are interested in exploring opportunities at Charlie Health, please go directly to our Careers Page: https://www.charliehealth.com/careers/current-openings. Charlie Health will never ask you to pay a fee or download software as part of the interview process with our company. In addition, Charlie Health will not ask for your personal banking information until you have signed an offer of employment and completed onboarding paperwork that is provided by our People Operations team. All communications with Charlie Health Talent and People Operations professionals will only be sent from @charliehealth.com email addresses. Legitimate emails will never originate from gmail.com, yahoo.com, or other commercial email services.

Recruiting agencies, please do not submit unsolicited referrals for this or any open role. We have a roster of agencies with whom we partner, and we will not pay any fee associated with unsolicited referrals.

At Charlie Health, we value being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strive to cultivate an environment where individuals can be their authentic selves. Being an Equal Opportunity Employer means every member of our team feels as though they are supported and belong. We value diverse perspectives to help us provide essential mental health and substance use disorder treatments to all young people.

Charlie Health applicants are assessed solely on their qualifications for the role, without regard to disability or need for accommodation.

By clicking "Submit application" below, you agree to Charlie Health's Privacy Policy and Terms of Service.

By submitting your application, you agree to receive SMS messages from Charlie Health regarding your application. Message and data rates may apply. Message frequency varies. You can reply STOP to opt out at any time. For help, reply HELP

job_description:
#WeAreParamount on a mission to unleash the power of content… you in?

We've got the brands, we've got the stars, we've got the power to achieve our mission to entertain the planet – now all we're missing is… YOU! Becoming a part of Paramount means joining a team of passionate people who not only recognize the power of content but also enjoy a touch of fun and uniqueness. Together, we co-create moments that matter – both for our audiences and our employees – and aim to leave a positive mark on culture.

Overview:

The Paramount+ Creative Marketing Team seeks a Sports Copywriter to write, proofread and edit various assets that position Paramount+ as a leader in premium sports content with various audiences.

Responsibilities:

• Craft text copy for marquees and show pages.

• Write subject lines, pre-headers and body copy for various subscriber newsletters, as well as non-subscriber communications.

• Create performance, paid social and affiliate emails for various campaigns, series and live games and sporting events.

• Write audio ad copy for priority games and matches, with a proficient focus on UFC numbered fights.

• Create new and engaging strategies to communicate with our audience from push notifications to in-app messages.

• Help occasionally with additional marketing non-sports related requests, including marketing copy for News, CBS, and Paramount+ Originals.

• Editorialize and use storytelling to engage with fans about their favorite teams, coaches and rivalries.

Basic Qualifications:

• 5+ years' demonstrated experience (not including internships) in copywriting, editing, proofreading, media and marketing.

• Experience with working within a brand guide and AP Style guidelines, as well as the ability to apply, maintain and application style guidelines.

• Reports on-site five days a week either in NYC or LA office.

• Minimal travel required.

Additional Qualifications:

• Deep interest for all things sports, especially UFC, UEFA, NFL, PGA, college football and basketball.

• Highly organized collaborator who thrives in dynamic environments, juggling multiple deadlines across diverse teams.

• Meticulous attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy and quality.

• Proficient writer and communicator with the ability to convey ideas clearly, confidently, and appropriately.

Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) is a leading global media and entertainment company that creates premium content and experiences for audiences worldwide. Driven by iconic studios, networks and streaming services, Paramount's portfolio of consumer brands includes CBS, Showtime Networks, Paramount Pictures, Nickelodeon, MTV, Comedy Central, BET, Paramount+, and Pluto TV, among others. Paramount delivers the largest share of the US television audience and boasts one of the industry's most important and extensive libraries of TV and film titles. In addition to offering innovative streaming services and digital video products, the company provides powerful capabilities in production, distribution and advertising solutions.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

Hiring Salary Range: $56,000.00 - 80,000.00.

The hiring salary range for this position applies to New York City, California, Colorado, Washington state, and most other geographies. Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and PTO or, if applicable, as otherwise dictated by the appropriate Collective Bargaining Agreement.

What We Offer:

• Attractive compensation and comprehensive benefits packages. Check out our full list of benefits here: https://www.paramount.com/careers/benefits

• Generous paid time off.

• An exciting and fulfilling opportunity to be part of one of Paramount's most dynamic teams.

• Opportunities for both on-site and virtual engagement events.

• Unique opportunities to make meaningful connections and build a vibrant community, both inside and outside the workplace.

• Explore life at Paramount: https://www.paramount.com/careers/life-at-paramount

Paramount is an equal opportunity employer (EOE) including disability/vet.

Note: Posting is subject to change so please refer to career site for latest availability (SBJ-G337).

job_description: Are you ready to transform your passion for words into a creative career from the comfort of your own home? Imagine crafting captivating stories and compelling copy that leaves a lasting impression—all while sipping your favorite coffee in your coziest socks. As an aspiring Jr. Copywriter/Editor and Brand Storyteller, you'll embark on an exciting journey where every day offers a new canvas for your imagination. This role invites you to play with language, shape narratives, and breathe life into ideas, making brand identities shine. No prior experience? No problem! What matters most is your enthusiasm and willingness to learn and grow. You’ll be surrounded by a supportive network of professionals eager to guide and inspire you. Picture collaborating in a virtual space where your voice is valued, and your creativity knows no bounds. Here, you’ll master the art of persuasive communication, hone your editing skills, and unlock the secrets of storytelling that resonate. Whether it’s crafting snappy social media posts, refining web content, or shaping brand narratives, each task is an opportunity to flex your creative muscles. This role is more than just a job; it’s a gateway to discovering your potential as a storyteller in the digital age. Ready to put pen to paper (or fingers to keyboard) and start your journey? job_title: Senior Copywriter, Copy Editor job at Concord USA in US National
job_description:
Title: Senior Copywriter/Copy Editor

Location: Multiple Cities/States, MN

Job Description:

Duration: 6-month contract.
Hours: Part‑time (20 hrs/week) with full‑time (40/hrs/week) option. Must be able to work the US timezone.
Location: Remote (US-based candidates only).
Compensation: $45/hr W2 hourly or $60 C2C.

About Us

Concord isn't your typical consulting firm; we are an execution company with a passion for making things happen. Our mission is to help clients enhance customer experiences, optimize operations, and revolutionize their product offerings through seamless integration, optimization, and activation of technology and data.

We are purpose-built, merging the industry’s top specialty companies to amplify our Innovation Capabilities in analytics & AI, data management & engineering, UX and digital experience, and technical platform integration, automation & security engineering.

About the Role
We are seeking senior‑level Copywriting and Copy Editing talent to join our team for a 6‑month engagement to support a Fortune 500 insurance client.

This engagement is open to full-time or part-time hours and may be fulfilled by:

A part-time Senior Copywriter and part-time Senior Copy Editor
Or a full-time Senior Copywriter/Editor who is confident in owning both writing and copy editing.

This role requires a seasoned, self‑directed professional who can operate independently, interface directly with client stakeholders, and deliver high‑quality, compliant content within a regulated environment.

What You’ll Be Doing

Copywriting responsibilities:

Develop clear, compelling, and compliant copy across multiple channels (digital, campaign, product, and marketing communications).
Translate complex or technical subject matter into accessible, customer‑friendly language.
Adapt tone and voice to align with brand and audience needs.
Support SEO‑driven and performance‑oriented content initiatives as applicable.
Partner closely with editors and compliance stakeholders to refine content.

Copy editing responsibilities:

Serve as the quality gatekeeper for all written content.
Edit and proofread materials for grammar, spelling, punctuation, syntax, tone, and brand consistency.
Ensure adherence to regulatory, legal, and compliance standards in a highly regulated industry.
Fact‑check claims, disclosures, statistics, and product details.
Enforce editorial standards, style guides, and terminology consistently across deliverables.
Refine or support editorial QA processes as needed.

What We’re Looking For
Required Qualifications:

5+ years of professional experience as a Copywriter and/or Copy Editor.
Strong portfolio or work samples demonstrating marketing or communications experience.
Demonstrated ability to work autonomously in a client-facing capacity, managing relationships and expectations without daily oversight.
Proven ability to work independently in a client‑facing role.
Experience working within an agency environment.
Expert command of grammar, AP style, and brand voice adaptation.

job_description:
Copywriting Jobs No Experience – Become a Remote Copywriter and Earn $25-$35/hr

Job Overview

Are you interested in becoming a copywriter but don’t have prior experience? This is your opportunity to launch a remote career as a Remote Copywriter, earning $25-$35 per hour from the comfort of your home. Whether you have a passion for writing or are simply looking for a creative and flexible job, this position is perfect for individuals looking to break into the industry. No previous experience is necessary, as full training will be provided. As a Remote Copywriter, you’ll write engaging and persuasive copy for a variety of clients, helping them communicate their messages clearly and effectively.

This role allows you to work independently, with the flexibility to set your own schedule. You’ll receive writing assignments that could range from creating product descriptions to crafting marketing emails, blog posts, or website copy. Your ability to craft compelling, well-researched content... will be key to your success in this role.

Key Responsibilities
• Writing Assignments: Produce high-quality, engaging copy for various projects, including product descriptions, blogs, social media content, and more.
• Research: Conduct research to ensure accuracy and depth in your writing.
• Editing and Proofreading: Ensure that your work is polished, error-free, and aligns with the client’s goals.
• Collaboration: Work with editors and project managers to refine content and meet client expectations.
• Ongoing Learning: Stay up to date with copywriting best practices, SEO techniques, and writing trends.

Who You Are
• Passionate About Writing: You love writing and are eager to develop your skills in the field of copywriting.
• Strong Communicator: You can express ideas clearly and concisely in writing.
• Detail-Oriented: You take pride in producing polished, error-free work.
• Self-Motivated: Working remotely requires discipline and focus, especially when juggling multiple writing assignments.
• Tech-Savvy: Familiarity with word processing tools and research techniques is essential.

Benefits
• Flexible Work Environment: Work from home and set your own hours, allowing you to work when you’re most productive.
• Competitive Pay: Earn $25-$35 per hour for your writing projects.
• Skill Development: Gain valuable experience in copywriting, editing, and SEO, which will enhance your portfolio and open doors to future opportunities.
• Career Growth: As you gain experience, you may have the opportunity to take on larger projects or specialize in a specific area of copywriting, such as content marketing or technical writing.

Challenges You May Face
• Meeting Deadlines: Writing projects can vary in length and complexity, requiring good time management to meet deadlines.
• Adapting to Feedback: Editors and clients may provide feedback that requires revisions, so being open to constructive criticism is essential.
• Building a Portfolio: As a beginner, you’ll need to build a portfolio of work, which may involve taking on a variety of projects.

Keys to Success in Remote Work

To succeed as a Remote Copywriter, you’ll need strong writing skills, creativity, and a willingness to learn. Working remotely requires self-discipline and the ability to manage your own time effectively. Communication with clients and editors is crucial to ensure that your writing meets the desired goals. Staying updated on writing trends and SEO best practices will help you deliver high-quality content that drives results.

How to Apply

Ready to kickstart your copywriting career? Click the "Apply Now" button below to start earning $25-$35 per hour as a Remote Copywriter. No previous experience is required, and full training is provided

job_description:
Copywriting Jobs No Experience – Become a Remote Copywriter and Earn $25-$35/hr

Job Overview

Are you interested in becoming a copywriter but don’t have prior experience? This is your opportunity to launch a remote career as a Remote Copywriter, earning $25-$35 per hour from the comfort of your home. Whether you have a passion for writing or are simply looking for a creative and flexible job, this position is perfect for individuals looking to break into the industry. No previous experience is necessary, as full training will be provided. As a Remote Copywriter, you’ll write engaging and persuasive copy for a variety of clients, helping them communicate their messages clearly and effectively.

This role allows you to work independently, with the flexibility to set your own schedule. You’ll receive writing assignments that could range from creating product descriptions to crafting marketing emails, blog posts, or website copy. Your ability to craft compelling, well-researched content... will be key to your success in this role.

Key Responsibilities
• Writing Assignments: Produce high-quality, engaging copy for various projects, including product descriptions, blogs, social media content, and more.
• Research: Conduct research to ensure accuracy and depth in your writing.
• Editing and Proofreading: Ensure that your work is polished, error-free, and aligns with the client’s goals.
• Collaboration: Work with editors and project managers to refine content and meet client expectations.
• Ongoing Learning: Stay up to date with copywriting best practices, SEO techniques, and writing trends.

Who You Are
• Passionate About Writing: You love writing and are eager to develop your skills in the field of copywriting.
• Strong Communicator: You can express ideas clearly and concisely in writing.
• Detail-Oriented: You take pride in producing polished, error-free work.
• Self-Motivated: Working remotely requires discipline and focus, especially when juggling multiple writing assignments.
• Tech-Savvy: Familiarity with word processing tools and research techniques is essential.

Benefits
• Flexible Work Environment: Work from home and set your own hours, allowing you to work when you’re most productive.
• Competitive Pay: Earn $25-$35 per hour for your writing projects.
• Skill Development: Gain valuable experience in copywriting, editing, and SEO, which will enhance your portfolio and open doors to future opportunities.
• Career Growth: As you gain experience, you may have the opportunity to take on larger projects or specialize in a specific area of copywriting, such as content marketing or technical writing.

Challenges You May Face
• Meeting Deadlines: Writing projects can vary in length and complexity, requiring good time management to meet deadlines.
• Adapting to Feedback: Editors and clients may provide feedback that requires revisions, so being open to constructive criticism is essential.
• Building a Portfolio: As a beginner, you’ll need to build a portfolio of work, which may involve taking on a variety of projects.

Keys to Success in Remote Work

To succeed as a Remote Copywriter, you’ll need strong writing skills, creativity, and a willingness to learn. Working remotely requires self-discipline and the ability to manage your own time effectively. Communication with clients and editors is crucial to ensure that your writing meets the desired goals. Staying updated on writing trends and SEO best practices will help you deliver high-quality content that drives results.

How to Apply

Ready to kickstart your copywriting career? Click the "Apply Now" button below to start earning $25-$35 per hour as a Remote Copywriter. No previous experience is required, and full training is provided

job_description:
Click Here to apply directly : https://bit.ly/48PPJhS

Candidates applying with above link will be considered

Our company is seeking individuals to participate in National & Local Paid Focus Groups, Clinical Trials, and Phone Interviews.

With most of our paid focus group studies, you have the option to participate online or in-person. This is a great way to earn additional income from the comfort of your home.

We need participants on the following topics:

Child Related (individuals with children 18 yrs or younger)

Cell Phones (Apple, Android, or Windows...Prepaid or Contract)

Entertainment (TV shows, movies, and video games)

Food (If you buy fast food, casual dining, or upscale dining)

Sports (Casual viewers and die-hard fans)

Electronics (Tell us which devices you prefer and why)

Pets (Animal lovers needed! Dogs, cats, and other pets)

Automobiles (Do you own, make payments, or lease your vehicle?)

Responsibilities:

Show up at least 10 minutes prior to discussion start time.

Participate by completing written and oral instructions.

Complete written survey provided for each panel.

MUST actually use products and/or services, if provided. Then be ready to discuss PRIOR to meeting date.

Qualifications:

Must have either a smart phone with working camera or webcam on desktop/laptop.

Must have access to a reliable internet connection

Desire to fully participate in one or several of the above topics

Ability to read, understand, and follow oral and written instructions.

Experience:

No experience required but must be able to work independently.

Job Benefits:

Flexibility to take part in discussions online or in-person.

No commute needed if you choose to work from home.

No minimum hours. You can do this part-time or full-time

Enjoy free samples from our sponsors and partners in exchange for your honest feedback of their products.

You get to review and use new products or services before they are launched to the public.

Educational Requirements:

High school diploma or it’s equivalent

Compensation:

$75-$150 (per 1 hour session)

$300-$750 (multi-session studies)

This position is perfect for those looking for temporary, remote, part-time or full-time work. The hours are flexible and no previous experience is needed. Apply now while spots are still available!

Apply Job!

job_description: Have you ever wondered what it's like to discover new mobile worlds every day? As an Entry Level Copywriter, you'll embark on an imaginative journey where creativity meets technology, testing diverse mobile apps and games from the comfort of your home. Embrace the thrill of exploring user interactions and crafting engaging content that captivates audiences. Each app offers a unique story, waiting for your creative flair to bring it to life. Dive into this adventure, where your words shape digital landscapes and engage users in unforgettable experiences. Get ready to let your creativity soar in this exciting, home-based role!Roles: In this role, you’ll help review mobile apps by installing them on your device and testing how they perform. You’ll look at ease of navigation, app speed, and reliability, then write short summaries of your experience to support future improvements.Contract length: No fixed term — take part for as long as it fits your lifestyle.Rate: $35 per hour.Skills/background needed: You’ll need a smartphone or tablet with internet access. The ability to install apps, follow directions, and observe small details is all that’s required. Around 5 hours weekly is recommended. No experience necessary — suitable for newcomers who are dependable and curious.Location: Remote role (United States preferred).This role provides a simple, independent way to get involved in remote work. You’ll offer practical insights that help app creators refine the user experience — without any meetings, deadlines, or pressure.If you’re available to start soon, apply today and contribute your voice to shaping better digital products.Apply Now#J-18808-Ljbffr
evaluation_reason: This is an entry-level app review role with minimal strategic copywriting, presenting a severe mismatch with your 13+ years of high-level experience and specific needs in core skills, content niches, and quality standards.
